# Epic 5 Retrospective — v1.0.0 Release Candidate Cycle

**Date:** 2026-04-24
**Facilitator:** bmad-retrospective (Amelia)
**Project Lead:** Armel
**Epic status at retro:** all 4 stories `done`; `epic-5: done` in `sprint-status.yaml` (already flipped via Story 5.4 code-review close-out); `epic-5-retrospective: optional → done` as part of this retro. Stale `epic-3 / 3-5 in-progress` entries flipped to `done` as retro housekeeping — Story 3-5 met its own "transition to done" condition (AC #8 E2E validation via Story 5.2 rc.3 + Story 5.3 v1.0.0 success).
**Previous retrospective:** Epic 4 (2026-04-23).

## Summary

**v1.0.0 LAUNCHED on npm under `--tag latest` at 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z** with OIDC trusted publishing, SLSA L2 provenance, `isPrerelease: false`, a linked GitHub Release, a reconciled hand-curated CHANGELOG entry, and cross-platform install verification on Linux + Windows + macOS. Epic 5 delivered the four stories that cut the release:

| Story | Outcome | Key evidence |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| 5.1 Pre-v1.0.0 readiness audit | Seven-file envelope expanded to 8 after `update.js` silent-exit-0 bug surfaced during review (sibling-sweep caught a bug the spec's recon missed); 6/10 pre-flight items (H1/H3/H4/H5/H6/H8) fixed in-story; R4/R5/R6 playbook caveats bundled; 4 dispositions recorded without code change (H2/H7/H9/H10); R1/R2/R3/R7/R8 deferred post-v1. 22/22 AC PASS. | `540fdda` audit + `7b84411` footer back-fill + `cd9c711` review patches; audit artifact at `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` (moved out of `docs/` to avoid Starlight dotted-slug mangling) |
| 5.2 Cut `v1.0.0-rc.1` + smoke test | **Seven-attempt saga.** The main-dispatch path in `release.yaml` had zero prior E2E coverage (Story 3.2's alpha cut took the `feat/release-workflow` "Skip main push" branch), so the first main-ref dispatch surfaced five distinct pipeline defects. Spawned Stories 3-4 (PR-auto-merge refactor, issue #198) and 3-5 (direct check-runs API poll, issue #202). rc.1 and rc.2 exist as release commits on `main` but were never tagged or published — zero npm/tag footprint. rc.3 PASS on attempt 7. | `3fc1f00` hand-bump + `14f2a69` sign-off + `21afcf6` hygiene patch; issues #198 + #202 both closed 2026-04-23 |
| 5.3 Cut `v1.0.0` under `--tag latest` | **First-attempt dispatch success** — 7m 1s wall-clock (well within NFR1's 20-min target). Release env gate approved 18:52:32Z; bot PR #213 admin-bypass-merged 18:56:30Z; `Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing` completed at **18:56:39Z** (the NFR6 immutability activation timestamp). CHANGELOG auto-gen block had an empty body (zero `feat:`/`fix:` between rc.3 and v1.0.0 — only `docs(release):` + `chore(release):`) and was reconciled with the hand-curated prose. | Run `24852899833` + `c0b2b67` reconciliation + `4c69f64` review patches |
| 5.4 Post-publish + cross-platform smoke | Authored **one new permanent reusable workflow** (`install-smoke.yaml`, ubuntu + windows + macos matrix). Three-leg dispatch cleared 46s wall-clock, all `1.0.0` stdout, all exit 0. **NFR9 60-min window missed** — elapsed `01:27` (27 min past target), classified `missed-target-non-blocking` since no live defect surfaced during the unobserved window. Code review surfaced a HIGH shell-injection defect via `${{ inputs.version }}` — fixed in PR #221 before the retro. Re-dispatch confirmed regression-free. | Run `24856988815` (Commit 1) + run `24858754152` (post-patch) + commits `5b17847` → `d0cddab` → `4e7405a` |

**Headline:** v1.0.0 is live, provenanced, cross-platform verified, and cleanly reconciled — on the first attempt for the actual v1.0.0 cut. The RC cycle did its job: five pipeline defects caught on disposable numbers before the critical path.

## What went well

1. **The RC cycle worked exactly as designed — five pipeline defects caught on disposable numbers.** Story 5.2 burned rc.1 + rc.2 as release commits that never reached npm or a tag. Every defect surfaced (branch-protection `GH013` block on bot-token push to main, `can_approve_pull_request_reviews: false` repo setting, GH partitions check-runs per trigger-event so `gh pr checks --watch` couldn't see workflow_dispatch-originated green checks, aggregated-vs-per-context error messaging, space-preservation in required-context names) was fixed on feature branches + merged before Story 5.3 dispatched. Zero production impact; zero npm dist-tag churn; zero rollback invocations. The `v1.0.0-rc.3` → `v1.0.0` transition ran on the first attempt in 7m 1s because the pipeline had been hardened by the RC saga. This is textbook RC-cycle value.

2. **Story 5.3 validated NFR1 with real wall-clock data.** Dispatch-to-complete at 7m 1s (v1.0.0) and 5m 4s (rc.3) — two data points of a rolling-10 window, both well under the 20-min target. Release-env gate wait (2m 45s for v1.0.0) counted inside that envelope. SLSA L2 provenance verified via `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 --json | jq '.dist.attestations'` — `predicateType: https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1` attached to the tarball. NFR4 100% provenance coverage: MAINTAINED across every release post-OIDC migration (alpha cut, rc.3, v1.0.0).

3. **Story 5.1's sibling-sweep caught `update.js` silent-exit-0.** The spec's Dev Notes recon stated "other commands exit 1 on failure" — code review ran a full sweep of `status.js` / `uninstall.js` / `update.js` and found `update.js` had an unconditional `process.exit(0)` at the function tail, mirroring the H1 `install.js` bug. Fix applied; scope envelope expanded 7 → 8 files with explicit disclosure. This is the Epic 4 pattern — "adversarial review on dense prose finds bugs that look like prose" — extended to the sibling-sweep case: recon claims must be verified, not trusted from the spec's prior-art section.

4. **Seven-file envelope held on the audit story despite the expansion.** Story 5.1 authored the entire v1.0.0 launch audit, the six gap closures, three R-caveats, and the CHANGELOG hand-curated prose — all in one Commit 1 (`540fdda`) plus the self-referential footer back-fill (`7b84411`). No drive-by edits. The expansion to 8 was explicit + review-driven, not scope creep.

5. **Story 5.3's first-attempt dispatch worked because the pipeline was hardened.** Story 3.4 (PR-auto-merge refactor) and Story 3-5 (direct check-runs API poll) both landed before Story 5.3 dispatched. The bot-PR admin-bypass-merge pattern matched rc.3's PR #209; the `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` step cleared in 0s because merge was already in place by the time the step evaluated. The empty-conventional-commit CHANGELOG block edge case (rc.3 → v1.0.0 had only `docs(release):` + `chore(release):` commits) was expected per `deferred-work.md` 3-3 and reconciled cleanly by AC 10.

6. **Story 5.4's `install-smoke.yaml` is a permanent reusable artifact.** Every future release — v1.0.1, v1.1.0, v2.0.0, any RC — can redispatch it with the new version tag and produce identical three-platform evidence with zero additional engineering. `macos-latest` was elected into the matrix on create-story per Dev Notes (A), converting a soft NFR9 manual-spot-check into a reproducible CI artifact. One-time authorship cost, permanent cross-platform floor.

7. **Epic 4 carry-forward held across all 14 commitments.** Story 5.1 verified Stories 4.1 + 4.2 merged to main before opening (Commitment 1). Story 5.1 imported H1–H10 + R1–R8 into Dev Notes at create-story time (Commitments 2 + 3). `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr divergence audited KEEP (Commitment 4). Task-1 live recon held 15-for-15 across Epics 1–5 (Commitment 5). Adversarial code review with Patch/Defer/Dismiss triage held 15-for-15 (Commitment 6) — now confirmed on audit artifact prose AND workflow code AND install smoke. Downstream-spec narrowing at current-story-review time (Commitment 7) held for Story 5.1 → 5.2 → 5.3 → 5.4 chain. Inline-CLI dry-run at story-authoring time (Commitment 8) — partial: the jq `.dist` shorthand in Story 5.3 AC 11(iii) returned null when run (extraction path wrong), caught post-hoc, not at spec time.

8. **Story 5.4's HIGH shell-injection defect was caught before the retro — not shipped to production.** Code review flagged `${{ inputs.version }}` interpolated directly into a `run:` block on `install-smoke.yaml:67`. Both Blind Hunter and Edge Case Hunter flagged it independently (dual independent discovery = high signal). Fix routed the input through `env: VERSION:` with `"$VERSION"` quoted in the script. Semver-regex validation added as companion hardening. Post-patch re-dispatch clean across all three legs. Pattern: **supply-chain-adjacent workflows get two review passes** — first pass caught 3 patches + 4 defers; second pass (on the patch commit itself) caught 2 more medium patches + 5 more low defers. The second pass is load-bearing, not ceremony.

## Patterns across all four stories

### Primary-code-path-first validation is non-negotiable

The five pipeline defects in Story 5.2 ALL lived on the main-dispatch path (`release.yaml § Push commit to main` and downstream). Story 3.2's alpha cut had dispatched from `feat/release-workflow`, which took the `if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'` "Skip main push" branch in `release.yaml:239-244` — the main-dispatch code path was therefore completely untested until Story 5.2's first dispatch. **Every workflow-code story whose primary code path is main-dispatch MUST validate via a main-ref dispatch, not a feature-branch dispatch, before being claimed done.** The cost of this discovery was ~6 hours of real time across 7 dispatches, two blocker stories (3-4 + 3-5), and three remediation cycles. The user-memory `feedback_release_pipeline_e2e_validation.md` already captures this. The retro sharpens it into a rule: **feature-branch validation proves the workflow can run; main-ref validation proves the workflow can ship**.

### Recon claims must be verified, not trusted

Story 5.1's Dev Notes stated "recon verified other commands exit 1" — code review's sibling sweep found `update.js` with `process.exit(0)` at the tail. The recon was wrong. Same class as Epic 4 retro's "do not silently reconcile drift" — the answer is not to trust the spec's prior-art section, but to re-verify recon claims at review time. **Recon = live evidence at Task 1, not spec prose at create-story.**

### Adversarial review on dense prose and workflow-generation code keeps finding real bugs

Across Epic 5, code review caught:
- `update.js` silent-exit-0 (Story 5.1, Blind Hunter sibling sweep)
- Verification snippet that breaks on any subsequent `main` commit (Story 5.3 HIGH patch: `[ "$(git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}')" = "$(git log main -1 --format='%H')" ]` — currently false whenever main advances; replaced with `git merge-base --is-ancestor v1.0.0 main`)
- Step-27 prose inversion in the launch audit (`github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' evaluated true, so the non-main branch is correctly bypassed` — non-sequitur; rewritten)
- HIGH shell-injection via `${{ inputs.version }}` in `install-smoke.yaml` (Story 5.4, dual independent discovery)
- Semver-regex validation missing (Story 5.4, edge reviewer)
- Matrix-line-number evidence stale post-R4/R5/R6 inserts (Story 5.1, Edge Case Hunter)
- NFR1 dispatch timestamp off by 1s vs GitHub API (Story 5.3, edge reviewer)

**Pattern holds 15-for-15 across Epics 1–5.** Confirmed load-bearing on (a) workflow YAML, (b) operational documentation prose, (c) audit artifact prose, (d) CLI help output — every deliverable surface that this project has shipped benefits from the Patch/Defer/Dismiss triage.

### Second-pass review on the hygiene patch surfaces more defects

Story 5.2's hygiene patch `21afcf6` received a Round 2 review. Round 2 flagged four additional patches (derogation para missing PR links, attempts-table self-referential causation, skill-count weasel-word, install transcript exit-code claim mismatched to fenced block). Story 5.4's patch commit `d0cddab` received a second pass that surfaced two more medium patches (`workflow_dispatch` semantic allowlist, identity-validation of `OUTPUT` vs requested `VERSION`) and 5 more low defers. **When the deliverable is supply-chain-adjacent (release pipeline, install verifier, npm-publish path), budget a second review pass on the hygiene commit itself.** The first pass audits the code; the second pass audits the fix.

### Scope envelope adherence holds when pre-execution ban-list is explicit

All four Epic 5 stories named their scope envelope in Dev Notes / Project Structure Notes BEFORE execution:
- Story 5.1: 7-file envelope named; expanded to 8 with explicit review-driven disclosure when `update.js` surfaced
- Story 5.2: `release-audits/` + `package.json` + `package-lock.json` named; no CHANGELOG or RELEASING.md edits (those belonged to Story 5.3)
- Story 5.3: 3-file envelope named; `v1.0.0-rc.1` → `v1.0.0 under --tag latest` section rename held in the same CHANGELOG+RELEASING.md+audit envelope
- Story 5.4: 3-file envelope named; zero repo-source code changes; only workflow + audit + RELEASING.md cross-reference

Pattern carried forward **15-for-15 across Epics 1–5.** Pre-execution naming is more effective than post-execution grep sweeps.

### AC authoring drift across four categories

Four distinct spec-authoring defects surfaced during Epic 5:

1. **Topology claims go stale when the workflow changes.** Story 5.2 AC 8(a) said "main advances by exactly one commit" — wrote for the pre-Story-3.4 single-commit path; Story 3.4's PR-auto-merge refactor made main advance by two commits (merge + release). Disclosed in Story 5.2 preamble + audit artifact, not formally amended.
2. **Foreclosure clauses collide with latent infeasibility.** Story 5.2 AC 9(d) wrote "If prompts don't accept empty-lines-as-defaults, that IS a defect requiring a new RC per AC 12" — the install UI's `required: true` multiselect cannot be driven by piped stdin (no way for AC 9(d) to pass by the literal clause it wrote). Reclassified as documentation defect via issue #211; honest label is "spec deviation, foreclosure overridden by pragmatic judgment."
3. **"Spirit" framing is a lexical stretch.** Story 5.2's audit used AC 12's "spirit" to cover pipeline-defect RC rollover (rc.1/rc.2 failures were all upstream of `npm publish`; AC 12's letter scopes to RC-content defects + npm immutability). The hygiene patch added strong negative-evidence (`git tag -l` + `npm view versions --json` both empty) that closes AC 12's actual contract.
4. **Char-count arithmetic errors.** Twice: Story 5.1 AC 21 (spec claimed 69, actual 53) and Story 5.3 AC 15 (spec claimed 64, actual 78). Implementers used the verbatim string both times; zero functional divergence.

**Decision (per retro framing):** fold corrections into next-touch, not a standalone retroactive amendment pass. The four categories are archaeological on dead ACs; the meta-lesson goes into Commitment 13 below. Specifically:
- AC 8(a) topology → resolves when Epic 6 next touches the audit
- AC 9(d) foreclosure → closes with issue #211 (which says the same thing)
- AC 12 "spirit" → revisit next time a release-pipeline story lands; the contract AC 12 actually cared about (npm immutability) was satisfied
- AC 15 char-count → dismiss; verbatim-string use meant zero implementation divergence

## Load-bearing inputs carried into Epic 6

Epic 6 is cleanup: Story 6-1 delete `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml`; Story 6-2 remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`; Story 6-3 remove `NPM_TOKEN` secret from repo settings. Epic 5 outputs feed Epic 6 directly.

### Epic 6 pre-flight items (inherited from Epic 5 reviews)

**(P1) — `rc` dist-tag still pinned at `1.0.0-rc.3` post-GA.** Source: Story 5.4 deferred (S54-D1). Users installing via `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge@rc` remain stuck on the release candidate. NFR6 forbids mutating the npm state mid-story; Epic 6 (or a standalone ops ticket) owns the decision. Options: (a) `npm dist-tag rm bmad-module-skill-forge rc`; (b) `npm dist-tag add bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 rc` (pin rc at GA); (c) leave as-is with a note in RELEASING.md explaining the artifact. **Recommendation:** (a) remove — aligns with "post-v1.0.0 the RC channel is closed; use `@latest`." Enshrine in `docs/RELEASING.md` so future cut-ops don't recreate the pattern.

**(P2) — GH Actions default 90-day log retention rots audit evidence.** Source: Story 5.4 deferred (S54-D2). `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` references run URLs (`24852899833`, `24856988815`, `24858754152`) as primary evidence. When GitHub prunes those runs (default 90d), the links die and the matrix-result tables become unverifiable. Cross-cuts every audit artifact that references a run URL. Mitigations: (a) upload summary artifact from `install-smoke.yaml` with structured JSON of per-leg stdout/exit/timings + retain for 400d; (b) copy raw per-leg stdout into the audit artifact verbatim (eliminates GH retention dependence); (c) increase repo-level log retention. **Recommendation:** (b) as a minimum — audit artifacts are the canonical record, so they should be self-contained rather than linking to soon-to-rot run URLs. (a) as a bonus for future releases. Address in Epic 6 or a standalone "audit-evidence hardening" story.

**(P3) — NFR9 needs automation, not policy softening.** Source: Story 5.4 `missed-target-non-blocking` classification. The 60-min window is effectively unachievable under solo-maintainer operation because it requires a maintainer-in-the-loop for manual `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch. **Recommendation:** add `on: workflow_run: { workflows: [Release], types: [completed] }` OR `on: release: { types: [published] }` trigger to `install-smoke.yaml` so it auto-dispatches in the same few minutes as `release.yaml` completion. One-time workflow edit; permanent NFR9 enforcement. Track as a post-v1.0.0 release-pipeline hardening story (new) — candidate for Epic 6 scope expansion or a dedicated follow-up.

**(P4) — Orphaned `feat/release-workflow` branch + `v0.10.1-alpha.0` tag.** Source: Epic 3 retro standing flag #15. The branch carries the orphaned commit `2a57dcbd` reachable only via tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` + branch `feat/release-workflow`. Do NOT delete the branch until GitHub retention behavior on orphaned commits is confirmed. Story 6-adjacent cleanup story is the natural home. **Recommendation:** verify reachability post-branch-deletion on a throwaway test case first (e.g. delete a test branch with an orphaned commit, confirm the commit survives via tag reference for 90+ days), then remove `feat/release-workflow`.

### Remaining standing flags (no fire date)

**(P5) — `prevent_self_review: false → true` on the `release` environment + revoke solo-maintainer ruleset bypass.** Source: Epic 1 standing flag, clarified by Epic 3 Story 3.3 AC #6 drift. Both flips needed together when a second reviewer onboards. Fires when the project grows past solo maintenance.

**(P6) — `install.js:23` silent-exit-0 fix pattern.** Source: Epic 2 retro #9, landed in Story 5.1 for `install.js` + extended to `update.js` via review. Remaining exit-path audit surface: new CLI commands MUST follow the "explicit exit per branch" pattern; add an ESLint rule or tools/validate-* check if this recurs.

**(P7) — `package.json.main` points at side-effectful CLI entrypoint.** Source: Epic 2 retro #10. STABILITY.md Path B handles the semantics (no programmatic API); the field itself is still a minor pre-v1.0.0 cleanup candidate that slipped past Epic 5. Addressable in Epic 6 scope if `package.json` is being touched for Story 6-2 anyway.

**(P8) — Cross-file anchor validator + `CONTRIBUTING.md` → `docs/` link coverage.** Source: Epic 4 retro (H10, I6). Currently no CI validator catches anchor rot. Couples with the P2 audit-evidence hardening if done together.

### Release-workflow hardening candidates (unchanged from Epic 3/4 retros)

**(F1–F6)** Release.yaml structural improvements — alpha/beta/rc substring classification + empty-$VERSION fallback; tag-push-before-publish reorder; zero-conventional-commit detect; Husky defense-in-depth; `release:` → `chore(release):` prefix; feature-branch alpha-cut orphan handling. Queued for a post-v1.0.0 dedicated hardening story — not blocking Epic 6.

**(G1–G4)** Supply-chain hardening — SHA-pin third-party actions; npm@latest pin; Node 20 deprecation; uv cache. Queued for post-v1.0.0.

### Release-pipeline deferred items (Epic 5 new)

**Story 5.3 deferred (S53-D1 through S53-D11):** doc-polish + audit-methodology + CHANGELOG hygiene items; cosmetic across RELEASING.md and the launch audit. Bundle into an "audit methodology polish" pass if/when the audit template is revisited.

**Story 5.4 deferred (S54-D3 through S54-D9):** concurrency group, step-level timeouts, strict semver regex, tarball-integrity check, stderr capture, TTY guard, `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` aggregation. All LOW; bundle into an `install-smoke.yaml` hardening pass if/when the workflow is next touched.

**Story 3-4 + 3-5 deferred (T9-D1 through T9-D12 + S35-D1 through S35-D6):** 18 items covering release.yaml race windows, timeout tuning, and diagnostic-loss edge cases. All functionally latent; hardening-story territory post-v1.0.0.

## Commitments

### Carry-forward into Epic 6

1. **Story 6-1 `create-story` MUST verify Epic 5 close-out artifacts exist on `main` before opening.** Specifically: (a) `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` with `### Decision: v1.0.0 launch status = LAUNCHED` (`:648`) + `### Decision: v1.0.0 cross-platform verification status = VERIFIED` (`:720`); (b) `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` present; (c) `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` present; (d) `sprint-status.yaml` shows `epic-5: done` + `5-4: done`. Without these, Epic 6 cleanup's "enabling precondition" claim in the PR description is unsupportable.

2. **Story 6-1 SHOULD also remove `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` from the HIGH shell-injection concern surface.** Both legacy workflows predate the `env:` injection pattern that Story 5.4's patch introduced. `manual-release.yaml` in particular uses `${{ github.event.inputs.* }}` in `run:` blocks without env-var isolation. Removing them — Story 6-1's literal scope — also removes that attack surface. Cite S54's shell-injection catch as additional rationale in the PR description.

3. **Story 6-2 `create-story` MUST handle the `release:*` script-deletion carefully wrt issue #211.** Story 4.2 prefixed those scripts with `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr + `exit 1`. Story 6-2 deletes them entirely. Issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility) is tangentially related — it tracks a separate install-UX defect. Do NOT bundle #211 into Story 6-2. They're separate tickets in separate territories.

4. **Story 6-3 `NPM_TOKEN` removal is unblocked ONLY because v1.0.0 shipped via OIDC successfully.** Document that precondition explicitly in the PR description — the secret existed as the rollback path during the migration; v1.0.0 launch operationalized OIDC as the canonical path. Cite Story 5.3's run URL `24852899833` + provenance URL as the "OIDC has shipped v1.0.0" evidence.

5. **Epic 6 cleanup sequencing: 6-1 → 6-2 → 6-3, no bundling.** Each story has a distinct risk profile (workflow deletion vs npm script deletion vs repo-secret deletion). Landing them separately keeps the rollback surface small; a post-6-1 regression doesn't require reverting 6-2/6-3 state. Previous epics have shown that separate PRs = separate reverts = lower blast radius.

### Process improvements (continue across Epic 6+)

6. **Primary-code-path-first validation for workflow-code stories.** Any workflow-code story whose primary code path is main-dispatch MUST validate via a main-ref dispatch, not a feature-branch dispatch, before being claimed done. Derived from Epic 5 Story 5.2's 7-attempt saga. Feature-branch validation proves the workflow *can run*; main-ref validation proves the workflow *can ship*. For Epic 6: Story 6-1/6-2/6-3 are file-deletion stories, not workflow-code stories — this rule doesn't apply directly. Carries to any future `release.yaml` / `install-smoke.yaml` / `quality.yaml` edits.

7. **Recon claims require live verification at review time, not just at create-story time.** Derived from Epic 5 Story 5.1's `update.js` silent-exit-0 discovery. The spec's Dev Notes said "recon verified others exit 1" and it was wrong. Code reviewers should re-run recon CLI on prior-art claims during adversarial review — not just against the diff, but against the state the diff assumes.

8. **Live `gh api` / UI / `jq` recon block at Task 1 of every story.** 15-for-15 across Epics 1–5. Unchanged.

9. **Adversarial code review on every story, with explicit Patch / Defer / Dismiss triage.** 15-for-15. Unchanged.

10. **Supply-chain-adjacent workflows get two review passes.** First pass audits the code; second pass audits the fix. Confirmed load-bearing on Story 5.2 (Round 2 on hygiene patch surfaced four more patches) and Story 5.4 (second pass on patch commit surfaced two medium + five low). When the deliverable is release-pipeline or install-verification code, budget the second pass. New for Epic 6.

11. **When a downstream story's premise changes mid-epic, patch the downstream planning artifact in the current story's review.** 5-for-5 across Epics 2/3/4/5. Unchanged.

12. **ACs that contain inline scripts or operational CLI MUST be dry-run at story-authoring time** (Epic 3 retro #8). Holds 5-for-5, with the AC 11(iii) `jq '.dist' {version, ...}` shorthand catch in Story 5.3 as the worked reminder — the spec's shorthand returned null when executed (attempted top-level extraction of nested keys). Unchanged.

13. **NEW: AC authoring has four recurring failure modes — (a) topology claims go stale when underlying workflows change; (b) foreclosure clauses collide with latent infeasibility; (c) "spirit" framing is a lexical stretch; (d) char-count arithmetic.** Apply to future spec authoring: (a) re-check topology claims against current workflow state at create-story; (b) never write "THAT IS A DEFECT requiring a new RC" without verifying the preconditions are actually achievable; (c) if the AC's letter doesn't cover a scenario, write a new clause rather than stretching "spirit"; (d) run `echo -n "..." | wc -c` on any verbatim char-count claim. Archived ACs stay as they are — the lesson goes to future stories. See Epic 4 retro #8 for the parent pattern.

14. **NEW: Downstream-story validation is the real done-signal for workflow-code stories.** Story 3-5's AC #8 explicitly scoped E2E validation to Story 5.2's RC cut — meaning Story 3-5 couldn't formally transition to `done` until the RC cut succeeded. That worked: Story 5.2 rc.3 PASS + Story 5.3 v1.0.0 first-attempt-success effectively proved Story 3-5's Wait-step refactor. **Pattern to keep:** when a workflow-code story's validation is downstream, name the downstream story + the downstream event + the close-out transition explicitly in the story's Completion Notes List. Don't leave the transition floating.

15. **NEW: Stale `sprint-status.yaml` entries are retro-housekeeping territory, not story territory.** This retro caught `epic-3: in-progress` + `3-5: in-progress` — both stale because Story 3-5's downstream-validation condition (Commitment 14) was met during Epic 5 but the sprint-status wasn't flipped. Retro is the natural forum: it reviews the whole epic lineage, not just the current story. For Epic 6 retro: sweep sprint-status for stale entries at retro time; flip anything whose done-condition has been met.

### Standing flags (no fire date)

16. **Flip `prevent_self_review: false → true` on the `release` environment AND revoke the ruleset's solo-maintainer bypass-list entry.** Source: Epic 1 carry-forward, clarified by Epic 3 Story 3.3 AC #6 drift. Both flips needed together when a second reviewer onboards. Unchanged.

17. **`install.js`/`update.js` silent-exit-0 pattern watched on new CLI commands.** Source: Epic 2 retro #9 + Epic 5 Story 5.1 update.js sweep. Add an ESLint rule or tools/validate check if a third instance recurs. Standing; not blocking Epic 6.

18. **`package.json.main` points at side-effectful CLI entrypoint.** Source: Epic 2 retro #10. Addressable in Epic 6 if `package.json` is being touched for Story 6-2 anyway; otherwise standing.

19. **Orphaned commit `2a57dcbd` + branch `feat/release-workflow`.** Source: Epic 3 retro #15. Do NOT delete the branch until GitHub retention behavior on orphaned commits is confirmed. Epic 6-adjacent cleanup story is the natural home.

20. **Cross-file anchor validator covering `CONTRIBUTING.md` → `docs/` links.** Source: Epic 4 retro (H10, I6). New CI tooling not blocking Epic 6; bundle with P2 audit-evidence hardening if/when that lands.

## Readiness assessment

| Dimension | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| Story completion | ✅ 4/4 done (5-1 / 5-2 / 5-3 / 5-4) |
| Live artifacts captured | ✅ commits `540fdda`+`7b84411`+`cd9c711` (5-1); `3fc1f00`+`14f2a69`+`21afcf6` (5-2); `c0b2b67`+`4c69f64` (5-3); `5b17847`+`cc2d3b2`+`d0cddab`+`4e7405a` (5-4); runs `24852899833` + `24856988815` + `24858754152` |
| Adversarial code review | ✅ every story; 15 patches landed across Epic 5 (4 Story 5.1 + 8 Story 5.2 R1+R2 hygiene + 8 Story 5.3 + 3 Story 5.4 R1 + 2 Story 5.4 R2) |
| Acceptance Auditor verdicts | ✅ 22/22 Story 5.1; Story 5.2 AC-envelope with explicit AC 9(d) reclassification (issue #211); Story 5.3 all priority ACs PASS; Story 5.4 15/15 PASS |
| Scope envelope adherence | ✅ 4/4 stories held — 5.1 explicit 7 → 8 expansion with disclosure; 5.2 + 5.3 + 5.4 exact pre-declared envelopes |
| Epic 4 retro carry-forward | ✅ 14/14 commitments held |
| NFR1 cycle-time data | ✅ 2/10 data points collected: rc.3 = 5m 4s + v1.0.0 = 7m 1s; both well under 20-min target |
| NFR4 provenance coverage | ✅ 100% — alpha cut + rc.3 + v1.0.0 all carry SLSA L2 attestation |
| NFR6 immutability activation | ✅ 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z — v1.0.0 is forever under `@latest`; rollback path is `npm deprecate`, never unpublish |
| NFR9 cross-platform verification | ⚠️ `missed-target-non-blocking` — 01:27 elapsed vs 60-min target; zero live defect; P3 automation recommended for Epic 6 |
| v1.0.0 published on npm | ✅ live at `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0` with `dist-tags.latest = 1.0.0` |
| GitHub Release created | ✅ `v1.0.0` with `isPrerelease: false`, linked from CHANGELOG |
| Sprint-status stale entries | ✅ 3-5 + epic-3 flipped `in-progress → done` as retro housekeeping (Commitment 15); `epic-5-retrospective: optional → done` flips at save time |
| Unresolved blockers for Epic 6 start | none — P1–P4 are pre-flight items for Epic 6 authoring, not blockers for Epic 6 kickoff |
| Deferred work tracked | ✅ `deferred-work.md` current (~60 items across Epics 1–5; +20 from Epic 5 reviews across Stories 3-5 / 5-1 / 5-2 / 5-3 / 5-4) |

Epic 5 is truly done. v1.0.0 is live on npm with OIDC trusted publishing, SLSA L2 provenance, cross-platform install verified, a hand-curated CHANGELOG entry, and a linked GitHub Release. The release pipeline was hardened through five pipeline-defect discoveries on disposable RC numbers before the critical path — the RC cycle did exactly what it exists to do. Epic 6 cleanup (6-1 delete legacy workflows, 6-2 remove release scripts, 6-3 remove `NPM_TOKEN`) is unblocked and can begin whenever.

## Next steps

1. **Create Story 6-1** via `bmad-create-story`. Import Commitments 1 + 2 + 5 into Dev Notes at create-story time. Scope envelope: delete `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` + `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml`; no other edits. Cite Story 5.3 run URL + Story 5.4 run URL as the "Epic 5 complete" precondition evidence.

2. **Schedule the NFR9 automation follow-up** (P3). Candidate paths: (a) add `on: workflow_run: { workflows: [Release], types: [completed] }` trigger to `install-smoke.yaml`; (b) add `on: release: { types: [published] }` trigger. Pick one during Story 6-1 authoring and file as a separate story under the Epic 6 umbrella or as a post-v1.0.0 hardening story. **Recommendation:** offer as `/schedule` for a 2-week post-v1.0.0 cleanup sprint so the policy decision (60-min automated vs 24h softened) gets revisited with real-world post-v1.0.0 release cadence data.

3. **Address P1 `rc` dist-tag policy** as part of Epic 6 or a standalone ops ticket. Recommendation: `npm dist-tag rm bmad-module-skill-forge rc` + enshrine the post-GA rc-channel-closure pattern in `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook` (or a new `§ Post-GA dist-tag policy` subsection). Not blocking Epic 6-1.

4. **Address P2 audit-evidence hardening** — copy raw per-leg stdout into the launch audit verbatim before GH's 90-day retention prunes the run URLs (2026-07-22 for the v1.0.0 runs). Low-effort; high-leverage for audit-artifact longevity. Bundle with Epic 6 Story 6-3 PR if the audit is being touched anyway, or standalone.

5. **No epic-update review session needed** — nothing in Epic 5 invalidates Epic 6 definition. Epic 6 was spec'd in `epics.md` (the single canonical workflow precondition) and Epic 5 satisfies that precondition.

6. **Close out issue #211** (piped-stdin install infeasibility) post-retro as a follow-up. Options: (a) fix the install UI to accept empty-line defaults for the multiselect (proper fix); (b) document in `docs/RELEASING.md` that piped stdin isn't supported and smoke tests must go through `install-smoke.yaml --version` (pragmatic); (c) leave open as "won't-fix rationale documented." Epic 5 retro recommendation: (b) — the `install-smoke.yaml` approach is already the canonical CI-side smoke test; `--version` covers the CLI-entry contract, and full interactive install has been validated on Linux via Story 5.2 rc.3. Full cross-platform interactive install is a maintainer-optional spot-check, not a CI gate.

156	## Dev Notes
157	
158	### What this story actually is
159	
160	A **pure workflow-deletion cleanup** covering one clause of FR19. Delete `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` + `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml`, and prune the three stale prose references in `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md` that assume those files still exist. Single commit, single PR, four files touched. The deletion is safe NOW because v1.0.0 shipped via `release.yaml` OIDC (Story 5.3 run `24852899833`) and cross-platform install was verified via `install-smoke.yaml` (Story 5.4 run `24858754152`, post-patch `24858754152`) — the OIDC path is proven load-bearing, so the legacy paths are provably redundant.
161	
162	**What this story is NOT:**
163	
164	- NOT a release. Zero `release.yaml` dispatch, zero `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch, zero npm state change.
165	- NOT a `release:*` script removal. Story 6-2 handles that. The `release:*` stubs keep their Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr + `exit 1` wrapper until then.
166	- NOT an `NPM_TOKEN` secret removal. Story 6-3 handles that. The secret stays at repo scope through Story 6-1 + 6-2.
167	- NOT a `docs/RELEASING.md` rewrite. Surgical pruning only — the three AC-7 edit points are the full scope; the rest of the 700+ line document is untouched.
168	- NOT a `release.yaml` header-comment edit. `release.yaml:4` says `Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml` — that is past-tense historical framing ("consolidates" in the present-perfect sense of "is the consolidation of") and stays. If the dev agent wants to pedantically tense-shift this to `Consolidated publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml (deleted in Story 6.1)` the edit is welcome but OPTIONAL; it does not count against AC 1's four-file list if skipped.
169	- NOT an audit-artifact append. `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` stays at its Story-5.4 close-out state. Epic 6 as a whole may append a `## Epic 6 Cleanup` block when Story 6-3 closes, but that is NOT Story 6-1's scope.
170	- NOT a second-pass review story. These are file deletions — the first-pass code review is sufficient (Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 10's "two review passes" trigger is supply-chain-adjacent workflows, not workflow-deletion commits).
171	
172	### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)
173	
174	**(A) Single-commit envelope, not two.** Story 5.4 used two commits because it needed to dispatch a workflow between them and capture ground-truth evidence. Story 6.1 has no workflow to dispatch — deletion is atomic and verifiable by post-merge `curl -sI` + `gh workflow list`. One commit keeps the rollback surface a single PR revert.
175	
176	**(B) Four files in one commit, not four commits.** Splitting deletions-from-prose-edits would land `main` in a transient state where `publish.yaml` is gone but `docs/RELEASING.md` still documents its emergency hatch — a documented trap for any operator reading the playbook during the window between merges. One commit means `main` never carries that inconsistency.
177	
178	**(C) `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet is deleted, not rewritten.** The bullet's sole purpose is to warn against invoking the legacy workflows; once they're gone, the warning is vacuous. Adding a replacement bullet like "these workflows have been removed" would be CHANGELOG-style narration that belongs in git history, not in a maintainer onboarding doc. The Story 4.2 precedent (which authored the bullet) is inverted cleanly — the deprecation warning is retired when the deprecated thing is retired.
179	
180	**(D) Scenario G emergency hatch is deleted, not reworded.** The hatch was a real operational path predicated on `publish.yaml` being dispatchable; that premise dies with the file. Keeping a rewritten "there is no emergency hatch" paragraph would be LLM-Optimization noise — better to delete the sub-bullet and let the reader infer from Scenario G's remaining Case 1 + Case 2 that PR-revert is the only path. A single short replacement sub-bullet noting "recovery is PR-revert per Case 2" anchors the inference without verbosity.
181	
182	**(E) `docs/RELEASING.md:431` historical reference stays.** The line says `Story 3.3 Patch A neutralized the legacy publish.yaml v* tag trigger` — past-tense historical context. Deleting it would erase the audit lineage that explains WHY `release.yaml` is single-rooted today. Story 6.1's deletion of the file doesn't invalidate the history of what happened to the file when it was alive. Keep.
183	
184	**(F) Shell-injection surface closure is a free benefit, not the primary justification.** AC 3(c) cites the `manual-release.yaml:59-61` HIGH vector as rationale-adjacent, not AC-blocking. The story's primary AC is FR19 (delete the files post-v1.0.0). The shell-injection closure is Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 2's "additional rationale" — amplifies the value proposition for the PR reviewer, doesn't drive the scope decision. If the legacy workflows were spotlessly secure, this story would still ship; the shell-injection vector just makes the ship more obviously correct.
185	
186	**(G) `gh workflow list` propagation lag is cosmetic, not blocking.** Epic 4 Story 4.1 retrospective D2 captured that GitHub keeps `state:"active"` for a period after file deletion (observed on `env-gate-test.yaml`). If AC 10(b) or 10(c) shows residual active-state at first poll, re-poll after ~60s and record both observations — this is expected GH Actions API behavior and does NOT block Story 6.1 close. The load-bearing verification is AC 11's `gh workflow run ... → not found`, which exercises the actual "can this be dispatched" question rather than the cached listing.
187	
188	**(H) Branch name `feat/v1-cleanup-legacy-workflows` follows Story 5.3/5.4 branch naming convention.** Prefix `feat/v1-` keeps the v1-era cleanup branches grouped together; `cleanup-legacy-workflows` is specific enough to distinguish from Story 6-2's forthcoming `feat/v1-cleanup-release-scripts` and Story 6-3's `feat/v1-cleanup-npm-token`. Do NOT reuse any Epic 5 branch (`feat/v1-install-smoke-workflow`, `feat/v1-post-publish-verify`, `feat/v1-install-smoke-review-patches`, etc.). Fresh branch.
189	
190	### Coupling with past, current, and future stories
191	
192	**Past (enables / blocks this story):**
193	
194	- **Story 5.3 shipped v1.0.0 via OIDC at `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`** — satisfies Epic 6 scope statement's "once the canonical OIDC path is proven by the shipped v1.0.0." Precondition for deletion.
195	- **Story 5.4 ran cross-platform install smoke via `install-smoke.yaml`** — three-leg matrix clean (ubuntu + windows + macos); confirmed no platform-specific regression. Satisfies the "v1.0.0 actually works" precondition empirically, not just by-virtue-of-shipping.
196	- **Epic 5 retrospective (2026-04-24) set Commitments 1, 2, 5** — live at create-story. Commitment 1 → AC 2 precondition set; Commitment 2 → AC 3(c) shell-injection rationale; Commitment 5 → no bundling with 6-2/6-3.
197	- **Story 3.3 Patch A neutralized `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag-push trigger** — `publish.yaml` has been inert since that patch (no automatic fire on tag push). Only `workflow_dispatch:` remains, which the Scenario G emergency hatch was the last active consumer of. This story is closing that hatch.
198	- **Story 4.2 added `DEPRECATED` headers** to both legacy workflow files (lines 1 of each). Precedent for the "retire the deprecation warning when the thing is retired" principle in Dev Notes (C).
199	- **Story 1.1 branch protection on `main`** — AC 9 PR-only constraint.
200	
201	**Current (this story):**
202	
203	- Four files touched: 2 workflow deletions + 2 doc prunes. One commit, one PR, one merge.
204	- No workflow dispatch. No npm state change. No secret change.
205	- Epic 6 flips `backlog → in-progress` at create-story time (first-story-in-epic rule). Stays `in-progress` until 6-3 closes.
206	- 6-1 walks `backlog → ready-for-dev → in-progress → review → done`.
207	
208	**Future (this story feeds):**
209

211	- **Story 6-3** (remove `NPM_TOKEN` secret) — unblocked by Story 6-1 close AND Story 6-2 close. Requires that no workflow on `main` reads `secrets.NPM_TOKEN` — Story 6-1 removes the two workflows that did; `release.yaml` uses OIDC, not the token.
212	- **Epic 6 retrospective** — sweeps sprint-status for stale entries per Epic 5 retro Commitment 15, reviews the three-cleanup-story arc's rollback posture, captures any emergent post-v1.0.0 patterns.
213	- **Next release cut (v1.0.1 / v1.1.0)** — inherits a single-rooted release surface with one release workflow + one install-smoke workflow. No operator confusion about which workflow to dispatch.
214	- **Any future Scenario G incident** — the playbook's recovery is PR-revert only; no emergency hatch exists. This is a narrower recovery surface but a cleaner one (everything flows through branch protection + code review, matching NFR2 + NFR3).

# Story 6.1: Delete legacy `publish.yaml` and `manual-release.yaml` workflows

Status: done

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

> **Precondition summary (2026-04-24 create-story time):** Epic 5 is `done`. v1.0.0 is live on npm under `--tag latest` with SLSA L2 provenance (`npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@latest version` → `1.0.0`). `main` tip is [`4e7405a`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/4e7405a) — PR #221 merge (Story 5.4 code-review patches). `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` carries both `### Decision: v1.0.0 launch status = LAUNCHED` (`:648`) and `### Decision: v1.0.0 cross-platform verification status = VERIFIED` (`:720`); the top-level `## Sign-off` is in four-sentence form closing Epic 5. `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` is live on `main` and has one green three-leg dispatch on record (run `24858754152`, post-patch). `sprint-status.yaml` shows `epic-5: done`, `5-4: done`, `epic-6: backlog` (flipping to `in-progress` on this create-story call per the first-story-in-epic rule).
>
> **What Story 6.1 is:** a pure workflow-deletion cleanup under FR19. The two legacy release paths (`publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml`) have been inert since Story 3.3 Patch A (tag-push trigger neutralization) and were marked DEPRECATED in their top-of-file headers by Story 4.2. v1.0.0 shipped via `release.yaml` (OIDC trusted publishing), closing the precondition clause in the Epic 6 scope statement: "once the canonical OIDC path is proven by the shipped v1.0.0." Story 6.1 now removes the inert files and prunes the documentation references that assume they still exist. No release is cut; no `release.yaml` / `install-smoke.yaml` edit; no npm state change; no secret/environment change.
>
> **Relationship to Epic 6 siblings and upstream work:** Story 6-1 ships first (this story). Story 6-2 (remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`) follows, Story 6-3 (remove `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret) last. **No bundling** per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 5 — distinct risk profiles (workflow deletion / script deletion / secret deletion) mean separate PRs = separate reverts = smaller rollback surface. Commitment 2 additionally notes: removing `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml` also closes a HIGH shell-injection concern surface — `manual-release.yaml:59-61` interpolates `${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}` directly into bash `case` blocks without the `env:`-isolation pattern that Story 5.4 introduced for `install-smoke.yaml`. The deletion is the fix.

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want the deprecated legacy workflows (`publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml`) physically removed from `.github/workflows/`,
so that the repo's release surface is single-source-of-truth (`release.yaml`), no operator (human or bot) can accidentally invoke an inert path, and the HIGH shell-injection attack surface carried by `manual-release.yaml`'s unshielded `${{ github.event.inputs.* }}` interpolations is eliminated in one stroke.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Scope envelope — one commit, four files touched.** Subject: `chore(release): delete legacy publish.yaml and manual-release.yaml workflows (Story 6.1)`. Touches EXACTLY four files:
   - `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` (DELETED — the file is physically removed from `.github/workflows/`)
   - `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` (DELETED — physically removed)
   - `CONTRIBUTING.md` (MODIFIED — prune the `Releasing` bullet referencing both legacy workflows as "do not invoke directly"; AC 6)
   - `docs/RELEASING.md` (MODIFIED — prune the Scenario G emergency-hatch subsection + Scenario G Prevention `id-token: write` expected-set + the `NPM_TOKEN § legacy residual` paragraph to no longer claim `publish.yaml` still uses the token; AC 7)

   **Verification:** `git show --stat HEAD` on the merge commit returns the four-file set with `D` for the two workflows and `M` for the two docs; `git log main --grep='Story 6.1' --oneline` returns exactly the commit subject above.

2. **Epic 5 close-out precondition verification — required before branch open.** Per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 1, the dev agent SHALL verify on `origin/main` at Task 1 time that every one of the following is true, and record the recon transcript verbatim in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References. If any precondition fails, HALT — Story 6.1 cannot ship without Epic 5 truly closed:
   - (a) `grep -c '^### Decision: v1.0.0 launch status$' release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` → `1`; the following line SHALL contain `LAUNCHED`
   - (b) `grep -c '^### Decision: v1.0.0 cross-platform verification status$' release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` → `1`; the following line SHALL contain `VERIFIED`
   - (c) `test -f .github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml && echo OK` → `OK`
   - (d) `grep -c '^### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest$' docs/RELEASING.md` → `1`
   - (e) `grep -E '^(epic-5|5-4-post-publish-verification)' _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` → both lines end in `done`
   - (f) `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/.github/workflows/publish.yaml --jq '.name'` → `publish.yaml` (confirms the file is actually on remote `main` to be deleted)
   - (g) `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml --jq '.name'` → `manual-release.yaml` (ditto)

3. **PR description requirements.** The PR body SHALL include verbatim:
   - (a) An explicit reference to FR19 — e.g., `Closes FR19 "delete publish.yaml/manual-release.yaml; remove release:* (Story 6.2); remove NPM_TOKEN secret (Story 6.3)"`. Story 6.1 satisfies the first clause only.
   - (b) The Epic 5 close-out evidence: Story 5.3 run URL `<https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24852899833>` + Story 5.4 post-patch run URL `<https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24858754152>` as the "enabling precondition" citation (the OIDC path shipped v1.0.0 cleanly, so the legacy paths are provably unneeded).
   - (c) The shell-injection-surface rationale: `manual-release.yaml:59-61` interpolates `${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}` directly into a bash `case` without the `env:`-isolation pattern Story 5.4 introduced for `install-smoke.yaml` (PR #221). Deletion closes this HIGH vector — per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 2.
   - (d) A four-file touched-list with one-line per-file purpose (matching AC 1 D/M breakdown).
   - (e) NO `Fixes #NN` trailer (there is no same-repo issue tracking this story; FR19 reference form is sufficient). NO `_bmad-output/todo/` references (per user-memory `feedback_no_issue_numbers_in_pr.md`). Cite Story ACs in `AC N` form (not `#N`).

4. **`.github/workflows/publish.yaml` — full file deletion.** The file is removed from the tree entirely; no tombstone left. `git show HEAD -- .github/workflows/publish.yaml` post-merge SHALL produce a `deleted file` diff for all 39 lines. Post-merge, `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/publish.yaml` SHALL return `HTTP/2 404`.

5. **`.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` — full file deletion.** Same shape as AC 4 for the 189-line file. Post-merge, `curl -sI .../manual-release.yaml` SHALL return `HTTP/2 404`. The deletion inherently eliminates the HIGH shell-injection surface noted in Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 2 — no additional env-var-isolation patch is authored because the file itself is gone.

6. **`CONTRIBUTING.md` edit — prune stale bullet.** The bullet at `CONTRIBUTING.md:86` reading `- **Do not invoke \`publish.yaml\` or \`manual-release.yaml\` directly.** Both are DEPRECATED (see each workflow's top-of-file comment header) and scheduled for deletion as part of that same post-v1.0.0 cleanup.` SHALL be deleted outright. No replacement bullet is added — once the files are gone, "don't invoke them directly" is vacuously satisfied. The surrounding `## Releasing` section's other two bullets (canonical path + `npm run release:*` fail-loud stub) are unchanged — Story 6-2 will revise the `release:*` bullet when those scripts are removed.

7. **`docs/RELEASING.md` edits — prune stale references.** Three subsections SHALL be updated so that no prose on `main` claims a file that no longer exists is still available or still uses `NPM_TOKEN`. The dev agent SHALL make surgical edits (pruning / rewording), not wholesale-rewrite the sections:
   - (a) **`§ npm Trusted Publisher § `NPM_TOKEN` is a legacy residual, not a safety net.`** (currently `docs/RELEASING.md:177`) — update the reasoning to reflect current reality: the token is retained ONLY because Story 6.3 has not yet removed the repo secret; `publish.yaml` is no longer a consumer of it (deleted in this story). Preserve the paragraph's essential message ("token is attack surface, not defence-in-depth") and the Story 6.3 cross-reference; drop the `(a) the legacy publish.yaml still uses it until Story 3.3 retires that path` clause (Story 3.3 is done; `publish.yaml` is gone).
   - (b) **`§ Rollback Playbook § Scenario G § Prevention`** (currently `docs/RELEASING.md:464-470`) — the expected `id-token: write` workflow-set comment SHALL drop the `publish.yaml` line entirely. Post-edit, the expected set is exactly two workflows: `docs.yaml` (GitHub Pages, orthogonal) and `release.yaml` (canonical). The `grep -l 'id-token: write' .github/workflows/*.{yaml,yml}` spot-check remains; its expected output shrinks.
   - (c) **`§ Rollback Playbook § Scenario G § Escalation path — emergency hatch`** (currently `docs/RELEASING.md:480-490`) — the entire `- **Escalation path — emergency hatch.**` sub-bullet block SHALL be deleted. There is no longer a legacy workflow to dispatch, so the escape hatch no longer exists. Replace with a single short sub-bullet noting that the recovery path is now exclusively PR-based revert of the offending workflow change (matching Scenario G Case 2), not an out-of-band publish. Keep Scenario G Cases 1 + 2 (disabled / missing) intact.
   - (d) Historical prose at `docs/RELEASING.md:431` (`Story 3.3 Patch A neutralized the legacy publish.yaml v* tag trigger`) remains — it is past-tense historical context, not a current-state claim, and preserves the audit lineage. Do NOT prune it.

   **Verification:** post-edit, `grep -n 'publish\.yaml\|manual-release\.yaml' docs/RELEASING.md` returns ONLY the single historical reference at `:431` (Story 3.3 Patch A context). Every other mention is gone.

8. **Quality gate (`npm run quality`) passes before PR open.** All 13 subcommands SHALL exit 0. Main failure vectors to watch:
   - `lint:md` on the edited `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md` — verify ATX-style headings, balanced fences, URLs wrapped in `<…>` where needed (MD034). The Scenario G prevention grep example is a fenced code block — keep it fenced.
   - `validate:refs` on `src/` files — unaffected (tool scans only `src/` per `tools/validate-file-refs.js:38`); workflow-file deletions don't trip it.
   - `docs:validate-links` — unaffected (tool scans only `docs/` site-relative `(/...)` links per `tools/validate-doc-links.js:23`); bare path prose like `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` is not validated.
   - `docs:validate-drift` — unaffected (scans `tools/validate-docs-drift.js` output against pinned SHAs in oh-my-skills; workflow files are orthogonal).

9. **Branch-protection compliance — PR only, not direct push.** Per Story 1.1 branch protection ruleset `Default`, direct push to `main` is blocked for all actors. The commit SHALL be authored on a fresh branch `feat/v1-cleanup-legacy-workflows` from `origin/main` at the latest post-Story-5.4-review-patches tip (currently `4e7405a`). The PR SHALL be gated by the 7 required status checks (eslint, markdownlint, prettier, validate ubuntu+windows, python ubuntu+windows). Self-approve OR admin-bypass-merge — match the Story 5.3 / 5.4 pattern.

10. **Post-merge verification on GitHub Actions UI.** After merge:
    - (a) `gh workflow list` SHALL NOT list `Publish to NPM` (from `publish.yaml`) or `Manual Release` (from `manual-release.yaml`). Both workflow names SHALL be absent from the output.
    - (b) The GitHub Actions "Workflows" sidebar in the repo UI SHALL NOT display either workflow name. (Note per Story 4.1 retro D2: GitHub can lag briefly after file deletion; `state:"active"` may appear for some time. Document any observed lag in the Dev Agent Record — but it is cosmetic and non-blocking for Story 6.1 close.)
    - (c) `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/workflows --jq '.workflows[] | {name, state, path}'` SHALL NOT list either `path: ".github/workflows/publish.yaml"` or `path: ".github/workflows/manual-release.yaml"` — once the GH API propagates (may take minutes). Re-poll if necessary; record propagation time.

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

A **pure workflow-deletion cleanup** covering one clause of FR19. Delete `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` + `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml`, and prune the three stale prose references in `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `docs/RELEASING.md` that assume those files still exist. Single commit, single PR, four files touched. The deletion is safe NOW because v1.0.0 shipped via `release.yaml` OIDC (Story 5.3 run `24852899833`) and cross-platform install was verified via `install-smoke.yaml` (Story 5.4 run `24858754152`, post-patch `24858754152`) — the OIDC path is proven load-bearing, so the legacy paths are provably redundant.

**What this story is NOT:**

- NOT a release. Zero `release.yaml` dispatch, zero `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch, zero npm state change.
- NOT a `release:*` script removal. Story 6-2 handles that. The `release:*` stubs keep their Story 4.2 `[DEPRECATED]`-on-stderr + `exit 1` wrapper until then.
- NOT an `NPM_TOKEN` secret removal. Story 6-3 handles that. The secret stays at repo scope through Story 6-1 + 6-2.
- NOT a `docs/RELEASING.md` rewrite. Surgical pruning only — the three AC-7 edit points are the full scope; the rest of the 700+ line document is untouched.
- NOT a `release.yaml` header-comment edit. `release.yaml:4` says `Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml` — that is past-tense historical framing ("consolidates" in the present-perfect sense of "is the consolidation of") and stays. If the dev agent wants to pedantically tense-shift this to `Consolidated publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml (deleted in Story 6.1)` the edit is welcome but OPTIONAL; it does not count against AC 1's four-file list if skipped.
- NOT an audit-artifact append. `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` stays at its Story-5.4 close-out state. Epic 6 as a whole may append a `## Epic 6 Cleanup` block when Story 6-3 closes, but that is NOT Story 6-1's scope.
- NOT a second-pass review story. These are file deletions — the first-pass code review is sufficient (Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 10's "two review passes" trigger is supply-chain-adjacent workflows, not workflow-deletion commits).

### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)

**(A) Single-commit envelope, not two.** Story 5.4 used two commits because it needed to dispatch a workflow between them and capture ground-truth evidence. Story 6.1 has no workflow to dispatch — deletion is atomic and verifiable by post-merge `curl -sI` + `gh workflow list`. One commit keeps the rollback surface a single PR revert.

**(B) Four files in one commit, not four commits.** Splitting deletions-from-prose-edits would land `main` in a transient state where `publish.yaml` is gone but `docs/RELEASING.md` still documents its emergency hatch — a documented trap for any operator reading the playbook during the window between merges. One commit means `main` never carries that inconsistency.

**(C) `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet is deleted, not rewritten.** The bullet's sole purpose is to warn against invoking the legacy workflows; once they're gone, the warning is vacuous. Adding a replacement bullet like "these workflows have been removed" would be CHANGELOG-style narration that belongs in git history, not in a maintainer onboarding doc. The Story 4.2 precedent (which authored the bullet) is inverted cleanly — the deprecation warning is retired when the deprecated thing is retired.

**(D) Scenario G emergency hatch is deleted, not reworded.** The hatch was a real operational path predicated on `publish.yaml` being dispatchable; that premise dies with the file. Keeping a rewritten "there is no emergency hatch" paragraph would be LLM-Optimization noise — better to delete the sub-bullet and let the reader infer from Scenario G's remaining Case 1 + Case 2 that PR-revert is the only path. A single short replacement sub-bullet noting "recovery is PR-revert per Case 2" anchors the inference without verbosity.

**(E) `docs/RELEASING.md:431` historical reference stays.** The line says `Story 3.3 Patch A neutralized the legacy publish.yaml v* tag trigger` — past-tense historical context. Deleting it would erase the audit lineage that explains WHY `release.yaml` is single-rooted today. Story 6.1's deletion of the file doesn't invalidate the history of what happened to the file when it was alive. Keep.

**(F) Shell-injection surface closure is a free benefit, not the primary justification.** AC 3(c) cites the `manual-release.yaml:59-61` HIGH vector as rationale-adjacent, not AC-blocking. The story's primary AC is FR19 (delete the files post-v1.0.0). The shell-injection closure is Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 2's "additional rationale" — amplifies the value proposition for the PR reviewer, doesn't drive the scope decision. If the legacy workflows were spotlessly secure, this story would still ship; the shell-injection vector just makes the ship more obviously correct.

**(G) `gh workflow list` propagation lag is cosmetic, not blocking.** Epic 4 Story 4.1 retrospective D2 captured that GitHub keeps `state:"active"` for a period after file deletion (observed on `env-gate-test.yaml`). If AC 10(b) or 10(c) shows residual active-state at first poll, re-poll after ~60s and record both observations — this is expected GH Actions API behavior and does NOT block Story 6.1 close. The load-bearing verification is AC 11's `gh workflow run ... → not found`, which exercises the actual "can this be dispatched" question rather than the cached listing.

**(H) Branch name `feat/v1-cleanup-legacy-workflows` follows Story 5.3/5.4 branch naming convention.** Prefix `feat/v1-` keeps the v1-era cleanup branches grouped together; `cleanup-legacy-workflows` is specific enough to distinguish from Story 6-2's forthcoming `feat/v1-cleanup-release-scripts` and Story 6-3's `feat/v1-cleanup-npm-token`. Do NOT reuse any Epic 5 branch (`feat/v1-install-smoke-workflow`, `feat/v1-post-publish-verify`, `feat/v1-install-smoke-review-patches`, etc.). Fresh branch.

### Coupling with past, current, and future stories

**Past (enables / blocks this story):**

- **Story 5.3 shipped v1.0.0 via OIDC at `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`** — satisfies Epic 6 scope statement's "once the canonical OIDC path is proven by the shipped v1.0.0." Precondition for deletion.
- **Story 5.4 ran cross-platform install smoke via `install-smoke.yaml`** — three-leg matrix clean (ubuntu + windows + macos); confirmed no platform-specific regression. Satisfies the "v1.0.0 actually works" precondition empirically, not just by-virtue-of-shipping.
- **Epic 5 retrospective (2026-04-24) set Commitments 1, 2, 5** — live at create-story. Commitment 1 → AC 2 precondition set; Commitment 2 → AC 3(c) shell-injection rationale; Commitment 5 → no bundling with 6-2/6-3.
- **Story 3.3 Patch A neutralized `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag-push trigger** — `publish.yaml` has been inert since that patch (no automatic fire on tag push). Only `workflow_dispatch:` remains, which the Scenario G emergency hatch was the last active consumer of. This story is closing that hatch.
- **Story 4.2 added `DEPRECATED` headers** to both legacy workflow files (lines 1 of each). Precedent for the "retire the deprecation warning when the thing is retired" principle in Dev Notes (C).
- **Story 1.1 branch protection on `main`** — AC 9 PR-only constraint.

**Current (this story):**

- Four files touched: 2 workflow deletions + 2 doc prunes. One commit, one PR, one merge.
- No workflow dispatch. No npm state change. No secret change.
- Epic 6 flips `backlog → in-progress` at create-story time (first-story-in-epic rule). Stays `in-progress` until 6-3 closes.
- 6-1 walks `backlog → ready-for-dev → in-progress → review → done`.

**Future (this story feeds):**

- **Story 6-2** (remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`) — unblocked by Story 6-1 close. The `release:*` stubs remain deprecated-with-exit-1 through that story.
- **Story 6-3** (remove `NPM_TOKEN` secret) — unblocked by Story 6-1 close AND Story 6-2 close. Requires that no workflow on `main` reads `secrets.NPM_TOKEN` — Story 6-1 removes the two workflows that did; `release.yaml` uses OIDC, not the token.

### Review Findings

**Method:** Three parallel adversarial layers — Blind Hunter (diff-only, no project access), Edge Case Hunter (diff + repo read), Acceptance Auditor (diff + spec + repo read).

**Summary:** 0 decision-needed, 0 patches, 2 deferred, 9 dismissed as noise. Acceptance Auditor returned `NO FINDINGS` across all 15 ACs. Two minor doc-hygiene items deferred to Epic 6 sweep — neither is caused by Story 6.1's scope (both pre-exist and are explicitly scoped out per Dev Notes (F) and AC 13).

- [x] [Review][Defer] `release.yaml:4` header comment still reads "Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml" [`.github/workflows/release.yaml:4`] — deferred per Dev Notes (F): spec explicitly marks this edit OPTIONAL (does not count against AC 1 four-file envelope if skipped). Past-tense tense-shift (e.g. "Replaces" / "Supersedes" / "Consolidated … (deleted in Story 6.1)") would reconcile. Natural home: Epic 6 retrospective sweep or Story 6-2/6-3 PR.
- [x] [Review][Defer] `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:728` still frames Story 6-1 as "unblocked" (pending) [`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:728`] — deferred per AC 13: Story 6.1 MUST NOT touch `release-audits/`. The audit is a point-in-time record written before 6-1 shipped, not a living doc. Epic 6 close-out may append a `## Epic 6 Cleanup` block per Story 6.1 Dev Notes "What this story is NOT" (item 6). Natural home: Epic 6 retrospective (per Epic 5 retro Commitment 15 sprint-status sweep discipline).

**Dismissed (9):**

- _Blind Hunter #1 — "grep muscle memory drift" for `publish.yaml` in Scenario G Prevention expected set_: LOW confidence speculation; operator confusion risk is vanishingly low and the edit is the correct shape per AC 7(b).
- _Blind Hunter #2 — "dangling Scenario G cross-reference elsewhere may exist"_: Edge Case Hunter verified — Scenario G's Trigger/Detection/Recovery/Prevention/Constraints sub-anchors remain intact; the cross-reference matrix row still matches the new PR-revert-only CLI verbs. No dangling reference.
- _Blind Hunter #3 — CONTRIBUTING.md adjacent bullet less-well-scoped post-deletion_: cosmetic only; the preceding bullet's "post-v1.0.0 cleanup pass" reads correctly in isolation.
- _Blind Hunter #4 — NPM_TOKEN claim "no workflow on main consumes it"_: verified TRUE. `grep -rn 'NPM_TOKEN\|secrets\.NPM_TOKEN' .github/workflows/` returns only `release.yaml:191` and `release.yaml:688`, both setting `NPM_TOKEN: ""` (defensive empty override to block fallback), not consuming `secrets.NPM_TOKEN`. Doc claim is accurate.
- _Blind Hunter #5 — no grace period for workflow_dispatch UI removal_: inherent to AC 4/AC 5 deletion intent; spec explicitly chose full deletion over deprecation-in-place (Dev Notes (C) precedent).
- _Blind Hunter #6 — Scenario G "no emergency hatch" creates potential deadlock if `release.yaml` broken_: design decision consciously justified by Dev Notes (D); spec chose narrower recovery surface (PR-revert only) for cleaner NFR2+NFR3 fit. Not a defect.
- _Blind Hunter #7 — "Every release threads through branch protection + code review + OIDC" absolutism may conflict elsewhere_: no actual contradiction found in unchanged sections; speculative.
- _Edge Case Hunter #1 — `docs/RELEASING.md:431` still references `publish.yaml`_: explicitly preserved by AC 7(d) ("past-tense historical context, not a current-state claim, preserves audit lineage. Do NOT prune it"). Edge Case Hunter flagged this without spec access — false positive.
- _Edge Case Hunter #4 — CHANGELOG.md historical entries mention "manual-release"_: expected and correct; changelogs are immutable historical records (flagged by the layer for completeness only).

**Acceptance Auditor coverage:** Verified all 15 ACs against diff + current repo state. AC 1 (four-file envelope), AC 4/AC 5 (full file deletions), AC 6 (CONTRIBUTING.md bullet), AC 7(a)/(b)/(c)/(d) (surgical RELEASING.md edits), AC 13 (zero-source-change scope) — all clean.

content.content:
1	# Story 6.2: Remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`
2	
3	Status: done
4	
5	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
6	
7	> **Precondition summary (2026-04-24 create-story time):** Epic 5 is `done` (v1.0.0 live on npm under `--tag latest` with SLSA L2 provenance since `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`). Story 6.1 is `done` — PR #222 merged at commit [`309f558`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/309f558) on `2026-04-24T12:55:35Z`; `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` and `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` are physically gone from `main` (verified via `curl -sI` returning `HTTP/2 404` and `gh workflow list` omitting `Publish to NPM` / `Manual Release`). `main` tip is `309f558` — the legacy-workflow cleanup commit. Sprint-status shows `epic-6: in-progress`, `6-1-…: done`, `6-2-…: backlog → ready-for-dev` (flipping on this create-story call), `6-3-…: backlog`, `epic-6-retrospective: optional`.
8	>
9	> **What Story 6.2 is:** a surgical `package.json` cleanup under FR19 (clause 2 of 3). The five `release:*`-shape script keys in `package.json:48-52` (`release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`) have been inert `[DEPRECATED]` fail-loud stubs since Story 4.2 (`echo >&2 && exit 1` prefix). With Story 6.1 having removed the legacy workflows they were warning about, the warning bullet in `CONTRIBUTING.md:85` and the stub scripts themselves are now stale-by-construction and can be removed outright. Two files touched in one commit: the `package.json` script removals and the `CONTRIBUTING.md` bullet prune.
10	>
11	> **Relationship to Epic 6 siblings.** Story 6.1 shipped first (workflow deletion, done). Story 6.2 ships this PR (script deletion). Story 6.3 (remove `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret) follows. **No bundling** per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 5 — distinct risk profiles (workflow deletion / script deletion / secret deletion) mean separate PRs = separate reverts = smaller rollback surface. Epic 5 retro Commitment 3 additionally warns: Story 6.2 MUST NOT bundle issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility) — that is a separate install-UX defect in separate territory.
12	
13	## Story
14	
15	As the maintainer,
16	I want the five deprecated `release:*` script keys physically removed from `package.json` and the now-stale warning bullet pruned from `CONTRIBUTING.md`,
17	so that the npm script surface reflects the actual supported operations, no operator is tempted to bypass the canonical `.github/workflows/release.yaml` CI path by invoking a local `npm run release:patch`, and the repo's release-retirement arc (Story 6.1 deleted the legacy workflows; this story deletes the legacy npm script entrypoints) advances one more step toward the single-rooted OIDC-only surface Story 6.3 will close.
18	
19	## Acceptance Criteria
20	
21	1. **Scope envelope — one commit, two files touched.** Subject: `chore(release): remove release:* scripts from package.json (Story 6.2)`. Touches EXACTLY two files:
22	   - `package.json` (MODIFIED — five `release:*` script keys deleted from the `scripts` block; AC 4)
23	   - `CONTRIBUTING.md` (MODIFIED — the `Do not use npm run release:* locally` bullet at `:85` deleted; AC 5)
24	
25	   **Verification:** `git show --stat HEAD` on the merge commit returns exactly those two files with `M` status; `git log main --grep='Story 6.2' --oneline` returns exactly the commit subject above; `git diff main~1..main --name-only` enumerates exactly `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `package.json` with zero additional paths.
26	
27	2. **Epic 6 precondition verification — required before branch open.** Per Epic 5 retrospective Commitment 1 pattern (live-recon at Task 1, not trust-the-spec), the dev agent SHALL verify on `origin/main` at Task 1 time that every one of the following is true, and record the recon transcript verbatim in Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References. If any precondition fails, HALT — Story 6.2 cannot ship without Story 6.1's deletion confirmed:
28	   - (a) `git log main --grep='Story 6.1' --oneline` → one commit hit; `git rev-parse origin/main` returns `309f558…` or a later SHA
29	   - (b) `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/publish.yaml` → `HTTP/2 404` (confirms Story 6.1 actually shipped)
30	   - (c) `curl -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/main/.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` → `HTTP/2 404` (ditto)
31	   - (d) `gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/package.json --jq '.name'` → `package.json` (confirms file present on remote; deletion target reachable)
32	   - (e) `jq -r '.scripts | keys | map(select(startswith("release"))) | .[]' package.json` SHALL list exactly five keys: `release`, `release:major`, `release:minor`, `release:patch`, `release:prerelease`. If any additional `release:*` key is present (e.g., `release:alpha`, `release:beta`), STOP and refresh AC 4 enumeration before proceeding — the epic's example list was non-exhaustive (line 750 "e.g., `release:patch`, `release:minor`, `release:major`, `release:alpha`, `release:beta`") but the authoritative set is whatever currently exists in `package.json` at Task 1 time.
33	   - (f) `grep -c 'npm run release:\*\|release:\* script' CONTRIBUTING.md` → at least `1` (confirms the AC 5 target bullet is present and matches expected shape)
34	   - (g) `grep -rn 'release:patch\|release:minor\|release:major\|release:prerelease\|npm run release' --include='*.md' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.yml' --include='*.json' . | grep -v 'node_modules\|package-lock\|CHANGELOG\.md\|_bmad-output/\|release-audits/'` — cross-check against AC 4 + AC 5 planned edits. Any hit not enumerated in this story's scope is a ripple the spec missed; record it and decide whether to include-in-scope (if trivially stale) or defer-with-issue-comment (if it lives in doc territory this story isn't authorized to edit).
35	
36	3. **PR description requirements.** The PR body SHALL include verbatim:
37	   - (a) An explicit reference to FR19 — e.g., `Advances FR19 "delete publish.yaml/manual-release.yaml (done in Story 6.1); remove release:* (this story); remove NPM_TOKEN secret (Story 6.3)"`. Story 6.2 satisfies the second clause.
38	   - (b) The enabling-precondition citation: Story 6.1 PR #222 merge commit `309f558` + `gh workflow list` output showing `Publish to NPM` / `Manual Release` absent. Story 6.1's deletion means the `[DEPRECATED]` stub scripts in `package.json` no longer have a canonical-path workflow pointer target from an operator's perspective — the stubs were warning against invoking workflows that no longer exist, so the warnings themselves are now vacuous.
39	   - (c) A two-file touched-list with one-line per-file purpose (matching AC 1's M/M breakdown).
40	   - (d) An explicit NOT-bundled statement: "Does not touch `NPM_TOKEN` repo secret (Story 6.3's territory). Does not touch `package.json.main` field (Epic 5 retro P7 standing flag; declined scope creep per Commitment 5). Does not touch `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md:123` transcript (historical record; same preservation principle as Story 6.1 AC 7(d) for `docs/RELEASING.md:431`). Does not bundle issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility) per Epic 5 retro Commitment 3."
41	   - (e) NO `Fixes #NN` trailer (no same-repo issue tracks this story; FR19 reference form is sufficient). NO `_bmad-output/todo/` references (per user-memory `feedback_no_issue_numbers_in_pr.md`). Cite Story ACs in `AC N` form (not `#N`).
42	
43	4. **`package.json` scripts block — delete five keys.** The following five entries SHALL be removed from the `scripts` object in `package.json`:
44	   - `"release"` (currently `package.json:48`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] Use .github/workflows/release.yaml via GitHub Actions. See docs/RELEASING.md.' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm run release:patch`)
45	   - `"release:major"` (currently `:49`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version major && git push --follow-tags`)
46	   - `"release:minor"` (currently `:50`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version minor && git push --follow-tags`)
47	   - `"release:patch"` (currently `:51`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version patch && git push --follow-tags`)
48	   - `"release:prerelease"` (currently `:52`, body: `echo '[DEPRECATED] …' >&2 && exit 1 ; npm version prerelease && git push --follow-tags`)
49	
50	   **Edit strategy:** delete the five lines outright. Do NOT add a tombstone comment — JSON doesn't support comments, and the git commit body already documents the removal. Preserve exact key ordering of the remaining scripts (the file is sorted by `prettier-plugin-packagejson`; `prettier --write` on post-edit `package.json` SHALL produce zero diff — confirmed by the AC 8 `format:check` gate).
51	
52	   **Verification:** post-edit `jq -r '.scripts | keys | map(select(startswith("release"))) | length' package.json` → `0`; `jq -r '.scripts | keys | length' package.json` returns the prior count minus 5 (pre-edit count is 32 scripts, confirmed by `jq -r '.scripts | keys | length' package.json` at create-story recon time; post-edit expected 27).
53	
54	5. **`CONTRIBUTING.md` edit — prune stale bullet.** The bullet at `CONTRIBUTING.md:85` reading:
55	
56	   > `- **Do not use \`npm run release:*\` locally.** Every \`release:*\` script (plus the bare \`release\` alias) is retained only as a fail-loud stub: it prints a \`[DEPRECATED]\` warning pointing at the canonical path and exits 1. The stubs exist to catch muscle-memory \`npm run release\` invocations before they ship anything; a post-v1.0.0 cleanup pass removes them outright.`
57
