gh pr create --base main --head chore/post-epic-6-releasing-md-polish --title "chore(docs): post-Epic-6 RELEASING.md polish (Story 6.3 deferreds)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Bundles the six deferred review findings from Story 6.3's adversarial code review (PR #224) into a single polish commit. All items were triaged `Defer` with "bundle into one follow-up PR rather than four one-liners" as the disposition; this is that PR. No functional change — prose and inline comments only.

Closes **Epic 6 retrospective Commitment 1** (docs-polish bundle scheduled same-session as retro save; retro at `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/epic-6-retro-2026-04-25.md`).

## Changes

### `docs/RELEASING.md` — four prose polish edits

| Site | Issue from Story 6.3 review | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `:80` | Table row's `see NPM_TOKEN note below` Ctrl-F path broke post-Story-6.3 when `:85` stopped leading with `NPM_TOKEN` | Cross-ref now points to `"No secret is scoped to this environment" note below` — Ctrl-F-matches the current paragraph heading |
| `:85` | Invariant stated but not auditable from within the doc | Added fenced audit-command block: `gh secret list | grep -ci npm_token` (expect `0`) + `gh api …/environments/release/secrets` (expect `{"total_count":0,"secrets":[]}`) |
| `:177` | Brittle `lines 191 + 688` line-pin into `release.yaml` with no CI guard — Epic 5 retro Commitment 13(a) "topology claims go stale" class | Replaced with symbolic anchor (`pre-publish dry-run step and final publish step`) plus a grep audit (`grep -c 'NPM_TOKEN: ""' … expect 2`). First instance of Epic 6 retro Commitment 2 symbolic-anchor convention |
| `:426` | Incident-response bullet had 404-ambiguity (operators under incident pressure couldn't tell `could not find secret` = safe vs broken scope) AND a duplicate `§ npm Trusted Publisher` cross-reference in the same bullet | Added explicit branching on expected `gh secret delete` outcomes (a/b/c); collapsed duplicate cross-ref to one reference |

### `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — two inline comments

Both `NPM_TOKEN: ""` FR4 defense-in-depth env overrides (`:191` pre-publish dry-run, `:688` final publish) now carry a `# DO NOT REMOVE — FR4 defense-in-depth …` inline comment so a future cleanup pass cannot silently delete them. Previously the prose at `RELEASING.md:177` claimed these lines "stay load-bearing" but nothing in the workflow itself carried that signal.

## Provenance

Maps to Story 6.3 review findings verbatim:

- `[Review][Defer]` brittle line-number citations at `:177` → edit 3
- `[Review][Defer]` `:426` 404-ambiguity → edit 4 (branching clause)
- `[Review][Defer]` `:426` circular cross-reference → edit 4 (collapse)
- `[Review][Defer]` `:80` cross-reference broken post-`:85`-edit → edit 1
- `[Review][Defer]` `release.yaml:191+:688` unguarded defense-in-depth → edits 5+6
- `[Review][Defer]` `:85` invariant not machine-checkable → edit 2

## Not bundled

- Epic 5 retro P1/P2/P3/P7 standing flags (post-epic housekeeping, not retro-polish).
- Issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility — Epic 5 retro Commitment 3 explicit non-bundling).
- Commitment 16 `prevent_self_review: false → true` (fires on second-reviewer onboarding, not now).

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run quality` — all 13 subcommands exit 0 pre-commit
- [x] Pre-commit + commit-msg hooks pass without `--no-verify`
- [x] `grep -c 'NPM_TOKEN: ""' .github/workflows/release.yaml` returns `2` (both sites preserved)
- [ ] 7 required CI status checks pass on this PR
- [ ] Merge cleanly; `main` fast-forwards
- [ ] Post-merge: re-run the `:85` audit commands and confirm both halves of the invariant still hold (`0` + `total_count:0`)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

message.content: The release does not update @docs/_data/pinned.yaml . Did you notice that?

file.content:
# Skill Forge — Pinned Canonical Anchors
#
# This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every version number, commit
# SHA, and library reference that the docs cite about oh-my-skills, the
# canonical SKF reference output.
#
# Every time a skill is recompiled in oh-my-skills, update the matching
# entry here and then run `npm run validate:docs-drift` from the SKF repo
# root. The script will:
#
#   1. Verify every skill below actually exists at the claimed version and
#      commit in $OMS/skills/<name>/<version>/<name>/metadata.json
#   2. Grep every docs/*.md for stale version strings and flag any that
#      disagree with the anchors
#
# If the drift check fails after a recompilation, update this file first,
# then fix the docs until the check is clean.
#
# Do NOT edit docs/*.md version references without updating this file
# first — the drift check will catch you. That's the point.
#
# File ignored by Astro/Starlight content collections because the parent
# directory starts with `_`. It is purely a build-time / CI-time anchor.

# SKF package version — matches the `version` field in package.json
skf_version: "0.10.0"

# Path to the oh-my-skills repo, resolved relative to this repo's root.
# Override at runtime with the OMS environment variable if oh-my-skills
# lives elsewhere on your machine.
oh_my_skills_path: "../oh-my-skills"

# Every oms-* skill currently shipped in oh-my-skills. The library key
# (e.g. cognee) is the user-facing library name; the entry key is the
# skill package name (oms-<library>).
skills:
  oms-cognee:
    library: cognee
    version: "1.0.0"
    source_repo: "https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee"
    source_commit: "3c048aa4147776f14d4546704f986242554a9ef3"
    source_ref: "v1.0.0"
    upstream_commit_date: "2026-04-11"
    test_score: 99.00
    test_mode: "naive"
    confidence_tier: "Deep"
    source_authority: "community"
    language: "python"

  oms-cocoindex:
    library: cocoindex
    version: "0.3.37"
    source_repo: "https://github.com/cocoindex-io/cocoindex"
    source_commit: "87c5dbf087bfccca83791861db0d33519ef09677"
    source_ref: "v0.3.37"
    upstream_commit_date: "2026-03-31"
    test_score: 99.0
    test_mode: "naive"
    confidence_tier: "Deep"
    source_authority: "community"
    language: "python"

  oms-storybook-react-vite:
    library: storybook
    version: "10.3.5"
    source_repo: "https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook"
    source_commit: "e486d3826bcd40c52db1c766966d1c8ec16df6cb"
    source_ref: "v10.3.5"
    upstream_commit_date: "2026-04-07"
    test_score: 99.49
    test_mode: "naive"
    confidence_tier: "Deep"
    source_authority: "community"
    language: "typescript"

  oms-uitripled:
    library: uitripled
    version: "0.1.0"
    source_repo: "https://github.com/moumen-soliman/uitripled"
    source_commit: "a5ffb45be05335d2c547436664cfbfb8c22d04df"
    source_ref: "HEAD"
    upstream_commit_date: "2026-03-22"
    test_score: 99.45
    test_mode: "naive"
    confidence_tier: "Deep"
    source_authority: "community"
    language: "typescript"

# Libraries that are mentioned in docs as illustrative examples but don't
# have a corresponding oms-* skill. The drift check IGNORES version
# mentions for these libraries — they're allowed to drift because they're
# illustration, not canonical.
illustrative_libraries:
  - better-auth
  - hono
  - drizzle-orm
  - spacetimedb-sdk
  - serwist

docs/getting-started.md:74:The installer reads the installed version from your manifest and shows the delta in the prompt — for example `v0.10.0 → v1.0.0 available`. Pick **Update** to replace SKF files while keeping your `config.yaml` intact. The option label adapts to the direction you're moving (upgrade, reinstall the same version, or downgrade) so you always see exactly what you're about to apply. Pick **Fresh install** instead if you want to wipe everything and start clean.
docs/RELEASING.md:202:- `<previous_good>` — the last known-good version immediately before `<bad>` (e.g. `0.10.0`).
docs/RELEASING.md:279:  > **Permanent-burn warning.** The version number is burned. After `npm unpublish bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1`, the next `release.yaml` dispatch with `version_bump: patch` on `0.10.0` will produce `0.10.2`, **not** `0.10.1` again. Attempting `npm version 0.10.1 --no-git-tag-version` would drive the workflow's `npm publish` toward an npm-burned version and return `403`. Treat the unpublish as a one-way jump in the version number line, not a true undo.
docs/RELEASING.md:588:- **Expected outcome.** `main` tip advances by 2 commits (the `release: bump to v1.0.0` commit on the bot temp branch `release/bot/v1.0.0-<run_id>`, plus the merge commit from the auto-merged or admin-bypass-merged bot PR); `jq -r .version package.json` → `1.0.0`; `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` → `1.0.0`; `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags.latest` → `1.0.0` (flipped from the prior stable, e.g. `0.10.0`); `rc` dist-tag UNCHANGED at the prior RC; SLSA L2 provenance attached (NFR4); GitHub Release `v1.0.0` with `prerelease: false` (NFR6 threshold — `1.0.0` contains no `alpha|beta|rc` substring); tag `v1.0.0` anchors on the bot PR's merge commit per Story 3.4 P9 (the tag is annotated, so `git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}'` gives the commit SHA to compare against `main` tip).

content.text:
Confirmed — this is a real miss. Here's the actual state:

**Stale:** `docs/_data/pinned.yaml:26` → `skf_version: "0.10.0"` while `package.json` is `1.0.0` and v1.0.0 is live on npm. The file's own header comment explicitly states "SKF package version — matches the `version` field in package.json" — so this is a documented invariant that's currently violated.

**Why nothing caught it — three-layer defense failure:**

1. **Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 readiness audit** should have caught it in the 8-file envelope sibling-sweep but didn't — the audit enumerated `package.json` + `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` as the version-bearing files and missed `pinned.yaml` entirely.
2. **`release.yaml` sync block** bumps `package.json` + `marketplace.json` on every release but doesn't touch `pinned.yaml:26` — the file was out-of-scope when Story 3.1 authored the workflow.
3. **`tools/validate-docs-drift.js`** cross-checks the four `oms-*` anchors (`skills:` list) and the `illustrative_libraries` list against oh-my-skills — but **never reads `skf_version`**. Earlier this session's `docs:validate-drift` green check (`OK: 4 skills checked`) was telling me the truth about those 4 skills and nothing about `skf_version`.

This is Epic 5 retro Commitment 13(a) "topology claims go stale" + Epic 6 retro Commitment 20 "cross-file anchor validator" — compound miss, both commitments proven load-bearing in one finding.

**Other `0.10.0` references in `docs/`** are all legitimate illustrative examples (getting-started.md line 74's `v0.10.0 → v1.0.0 available` is literally the upgrade-prompt example; RELEASING.md's `:202, :279, :588` use `0.10.0` as "the prior stable" in historical/example narrative) — none need editing.

**The fix has two layers:**

1. **Immediate** — one-line bump `pinned.yaml:26 "0.10.0" → "1.0.0"`. Tiny PR. Closes the current drift.
2. **Systemic close** — pick one or both:
   - **(a) Extend `validate-docs-drift.js`** to assert `pinned.yaml.skf_version === <package.json .version>`. Cheap (~10 lines); catches future drift at CI time but doesn't prevent write-side miss.
   - **(b) Add `pinned.yaml` to `release.yaml`'s bump-sync set** so every release atomically updates it alongside `package.json` + `marketplace.json`. This is the proper fix — same class of guarantee as the existing two sync targets.

Both together is the strongest posture: (b) prevents the miss on write, (a) catches it at CI if anything else ever sets the field.

**My recommendation:** do both in a single follow-up PR — they're the same feature (keep `pinned.yaml.skf_version` locked to `package.json.version`) from two angles. Want me to draft it now, or file as a standing flag for whenever you next touch the release pipeline?

Next action: you decide whether I draft the fix PR now (bump + `release.yaml` sync + drift-validator extension) or file it as a standing flag.

278:          NEW_VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
310:          PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$PR_URL" --json number --jq .number)
357:          HEAD_SHA=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json headRefOid --jq .headRefOid)
362:            --paginate --jq '[.workflow_runs[] | select(.conclusion == "action_required") | .id]')
363:          COUNT=$(echo "$STUCK" | jq 'length')
369:          for RUN_ID in $(echo "$STUCK" | jq -r '.[]'); do
393:          HEAD_SHA=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json headRefOid --jq .headRefOid)
399:            --jq '[.rules[] | select(.type=="required_status_checks") | .parameters.required_status_checks[].context]' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
404:          REQUIRED_COUNT=$(echo "$REQUIRED_JSON" | jq 'length')
413:            REGISTERED=$(gh api "/repos/${{ github.repository }}/commits/$HEAD_SHA/check-runs" --paginate --jq '.check_runs | length' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
432:              --paginate --jq '[.check_runs[] | {name, status, conclusion, started_at, html_url}]')
437:            # names. Unquoted `$(jq -r '.[]')` in a `for` loop word-splits on
442:              CTX_RUN=$(echo "$CHECKS" | jq -c --arg n "$CTX" '[.[] | select(.name==$n)] | sort_by(.started_at) | last')
448:              STATUS=$(echo "$CTX_RUN" | jq -r .status)
449:              CONCL=$(echo "$CTX_RUN" | jq -r .conclusion)
460:                  RUN_URL=$(echo "$CTX_RUN" | jq -r .html_url)
468:            done < <(echo "$REQUIRED_JSON" | jq -r '.[]')
530:            DECISION=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .reviewDecision)
531:            STATE=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r .state)
535:              MERGE_SHA=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergeCommit.oid // "unknown"')
536:              MERGED_AT=$(echo "$VIEW" | jq -r '.mergedAt // "unknown"')

130	          # via jq --arg to avoid shell-quote hazards.

132	          jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp
133	          mv .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
134	          # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
135	          WROTE=$(jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json)
136	          if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then

138	          fi

143	
144	      - name: Restore CHANGELOG preamble
145	        run: |
146	          # conventional-changelog-cli -s prepends the new release entry at byte 0
147	          # of CHANGELOG.md, pushing "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]"
148	          # anchor DOWN below it. Restore the canonical Keep-a-Changelog shape:
149	          #   # Changelog
150	          #   (preamble)
151	          #   ## [Unreleased]
152	          #   ## [<new release>] (...)
153	          #   ## [<older releases>] (...)
154	          # Idempotent: if "# Changelog" is already on line 1 (exact match), no-op.
155	          [ -s CHANGELOG.md ] || { echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md is empty or missing — aborting release."; exit 1; }
156	          if ! head -n 1 CHANGELOG.md | grep -q '^# Changelog$'; then
157	            awk '
158	              BEGIN { new_entry=""; preamble=""; rest=""; section=0; seen_title=0 }
159	              # section 0: new release entry prepended by conventional-changelog -s
160	              # section 1: "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]" anchor
161	              # section 2: first released version ("## [X.Y.Z]") onward
162	              section==0 && !seen_title && /^# Changelog$/ { seen_title=1; section=1 }
163	              section==1 && /^## \[[0-9]/ { section=2 }
164	              { if (section==0) new_entry=new_entry $0 "\n";
165	                else if (section==1) preamble=preamble $0 "\n";
166	                else rest=rest $0 "\n" }
167	              END {
168	                if (!seen_title) {
169	                  print "restore-preamble: # Changelog title not found in CHANGELOG.md — aborting" > "/dev/stderr"
170	                  exit 1
171	                }
172	                printf "%s%s%s", preamble, new_entry, rest
173	              }
174	            ' CHANGELOG.md > CHANGELOG.md.tmp
175	            [ -s CHANGELOG.md.tmp ] || { echo "::error::awk produced empty CHANGELOG.md.tmp — aborting release."; rm -f CHANGELOG.md.tmp; exit 1; }
176	            mv CHANGELOG.md.tmp CHANGELOG.md
177	            echo "::notice::Restored CHANGELOG preamble ordering (# Changelog → preamble → [Unreleased] → new release → history)"
178	          else

file.content:
- name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          # Targeted JSON edit scoped to .plugins[0].version — resistant to future
          # schema additions of other `version` keys. Uses a temp file + atomic
          # rename so a partial-write mid-step cannot leave the file in a broken
          # shape (jq has no in-place edit). The VERSION variable is interpolated
          # via jq --arg to avoid shell-quote hazards.
          VERSION='${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}'
          jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp
          mv .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
          # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
          WROTE=$(jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json)
          if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::marketplace.json version write failed: expected $VERSION, got $WROTE"; exit 1
          fi

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Restore CHANGELOG preamble
        run: |
          # conventional-changelog-cli -s prepends the new release entry at byte 0
          # of CHANGELOG.md, pushing "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]"
          # anchor DOWN below it. Restore the canonical Keep-a-Changelog shape:
          #   # Changelog
          #   (preamble)
          #   ## [Unreleased]
          #   ## [<new release>] (...)
          #   ## [<older releases>] (...)
          # Idempotent: if "# Changelog" is already on line 1 (exact match), no-op.
          [ -s CHANGELOG.md ] || { echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md is empty or missing — aborting release."; exit 1; }
          if ! head -n 1 CHANGELOG.md | grep -q '^# Changelog$'; then
            awk '
              BEGIN { new_entry=""; preamble=""; rest=""; section=0; seen_title=0 }
              # section 0: new release entry prepended by conventional-changelog -s
              # section 1: "# Changelog" title + preamble + "## [Unreleased]" anchor
              # section 2: first released version ("## [X.Y.Z]") onward
              section==0 && !seen_title && /^# Changelog$/ { seen_title=1; section=1 }
              section==1 && /^## \[[0-9]/ { section=2 }
              { if (section==0) new_entry=new_entry $0 "\n";
                else if (section==1) preamble=preamble $0 "\n";
                else rest=rest $0 "\n" }
              END {
                if (!seen_title) {
                  print "restore-preamble: # Changelog title not found in CHANGELOG.md — aborting" > "/dev/stderr"
                  exit 1
                }
                printf "%s%s%s", preamble, new_entry, rest
              }
            ' CHANGELOG.md > CHANGELOG.md.tmp
            [ -s CHANGELOG.md.tmp ] || { echo "::error::awk produced empty CHANGELOG.md.tmp — aborting release."; rm -f CHANGELOG.md.tmp; exit 1; }
            mv CHANGELOG.md.tmp CHANGELOG.md
            echo "::notice::Restored CHANGELOG preamble ordering (# Changelog → preamble → [Unreleased] → new release → history)"
          else
            echo "CHANGELOG preamble already at top — no reordering needed"

# Skill Forge — Pinned Canonical Anchors
#
# This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every version number, commit
# SHA, and library reference that the docs cite about oh-my-skills, the
# canonical SKF reference output.
#
# Every time a skill is recompiled in oh-my-skills, update the matching
# entry here and then run `npm run validate:docs-drift` from the SKF repo
# root. The script will:
#
#   1. Verify every skill below actually exists at the claimed version and
#      commit in $OMS/skills/<name>/<version>/<name>/metadata.json
#   2. Grep every docs/*.md for stale version strings and flag any that
#      disagree with the anchors
#
# If the drift check fails after a recompilation, update this file first,
# then fix the docs until the check is clean.
#
# Do NOT edit docs/*.md version references without updating this file
# first — the drift check will catch you. That's the point.
#
# File ignored by Astro/Starlight content collections because the parent
# directory starts with `_`. It is purely a build-time / CI-time anchor.

# SKF package version — matches the `version` field in package.json
skf_version: "0.10.0"

# Path to the oh-my-skills repo, resolved relative to this repo's root.
# Override at runtime with the OMS environment variable if oh-my-skills
# lives elsewhere on your machine.
oh_my_skills_path: "../oh-my-skills"

# Every oms-* skill currently shipped in oh-my-skills. The library key
# (e.g. cognee) is the user-facing library name; the entry key is the
# skill package name (oms-<library>).
skills:
  oms-cognee:
    library: cognee
    version: "1.0.0"
    source_repo: "https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee"
    source_commit: "3c048aa4147776f14d4546704f986242554a9ef3"
    source_ref: "v1.0.0"
    upstream_commit_date: "2026-04-11"
    test_score: 99.00
    test_mode: "naive"
    confidence_tier: "Deep"
    source_authority: "community"
    language: "python"

  oms-cocoindex:

    source_ref: "v0.3.37"
    upstream_commit_date: "2026-03-31"
    test_score: 99.0
    test_mode: "naive"
    confidence_tier: "Deep"
    source_authority: "community"
    language: "python"

  oms-storybook-react-vite:
    library: storybook
    version: "10.3.5"
    source_repo: "https://github.com/storybookjs/storybook"
    source_commit: "e486d3826bcd40c52db1c766966d1c8ec16df6cb"

    test_score: 99.49

  oms-uitripled:

# Libraries that are mentioned in docs as illustrative examples but don't

          VERSION='${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}'
          jq --arg v "$VERSION" '.plugins[0].version = $v' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json > .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp
          mv .claude-plugin/marketplace.json.tmp .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
          # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
          WROTE=$(jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json)
          if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::marketplace.json version write failed: expected $VERSION, got $WROTE"; exit 1
          fi

      - name: Update docs/_data/pinned.yaml skf_version
        env:
          VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
        run: |
          # Keep docs/_data/pinned.yaml.skf_version locked to package.json.version —
          # pinned.yaml's own header declares this as an invariant and tools/validate-docs-drift.js
          # enforces it at pre-commit time. This step enforces it at release time so
          # the invariant is upheld atomically on every version bump.
          #
          # Targeted sed: matches ONLY a top-level `skf_version: "X.Y.Z"` line. The
          # VERSION value is passed via env (not ${{ }} interpolation into the script
          # body) to avoid shell-injection on inputs per Story 5.4 defense-in-depth.
          sed -i.bak -E 's/^skf_version: "[^"]*"$/skf_version: "'"$VERSION"'"/' docs/_data/pinned.yaml
          rm -f docs/_data/pinned.yaml.bak
          # Sanity check: the written version matches what we set.
          WROTE=$(grep -E '^skf_version:' docs/_data/pinned.yaml | sed -E 's/^skf_version: "([^"]*)"$/\1/')
          if [ "$WROTE" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::docs/_data/pinned.yaml skf_version write failed: expected $VERSION, got $WROTE"; exit 1
          fi

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml docs/_data/pinned.yaml tools/validate-docs-drift.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): lock docs/_data/pinned.yaml.skf_version to package.json.version

`docs/_data/pinned.yaml:26` carried stale `skf_version: "0.10.0"` while
package.json + the live v1.0.0 npm release were at `1.0.0`. The file's
own header comment declared "matches the `version` field in package.json"
as an invariant — but nothing enforced it. Three-layer defense failure:
Story 5.1's pre-v1.0.0 readiness audit missed the file in its 8-file
envelope; release.yaml's bump-sync set included package.json + marketplace.json
but not pinned.yaml; validate-docs-drift.js cross-checked the oms-* skill
anchors and illustrative_libraries list but never read skf_version.

Fix has three parts, applied in one commit:

1. docs/_data/pinned.yaml:26 — bump 0.10.0 → 1.0.0 to reflect current live
   state. Also expand the file's header comment to document the two-layer
   enforcement that now exists.

2. tools/validate-docs-drift.js — add checkSkfVersionAgainstPackageJson()
   that reads package.json.version and asserts anchors.skf_version matches.
   Runs as part of `npm run quality` (local + pre-commit hook); not in CI
   quality.yaml (which intentionally excludes docs:validate-drift because
   it needs the oh-my-skills sibling repo checkout). Catches drift on
   pre-commit, with an actionable message that points at the release.yaml
   step name that should have fired.

3. .github/workflows/release.yaml — new step "Update docs/_data/pinned.yaml
   skf_version" after the marketplace.json step. Uses sed with a precise
   top-level-key anchor, VERSION passed via `env:` (not `${{ }}` interpolation
   into the script body) per Story 5.4 shell-injection defense-in-depth, and
   a post-write sanity-check grep. Adds docs/_data/pinned.yaml to the `git add`
   list at the commit step so the bump rides with package.json + marketplace.json
   in the same release commit.

Pattern: closes Epic 5 retro Commitment 13(a) "topology claims go stale"
for this specific invariant AND instantiates Epic 6 retro Commitment 2
"symbolic anchor / cross-file validation" as a tooling guard (not just
a prose convention). Release-side enforcement prevents future drift on
write; validator enforcement catches it at pre-commit if anything else
ever touches the file.

Test plan:
- [x] `node tools/validate-docs-drift.js` positive case — passes with
      "skf_version 1.0.0 matches package.json"
- [x] Negative-case probe — inject 9.9.9, confirm validator exits 1 with
      the actionable SKF_VERSION_DRIFT message; restore file
- [x] `npm run quality` — all 13 subcommands exit 0
- [ ] 7 required CI status checks pass
- [ ] release.yaml's new step will fire on next release cut (v1.0.1 or v1.1.0
      — deferred validation; no release in this PR)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head fix/lock-pinned-skf-version-to-package-json --title "fix(release): lock pinned.yaml.skf_version to package.json.version" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

`docs/_data/pinned.yaml:26` carried stale `skf_version: "0.10.0"` while `package.json` and the live v1.0.0 npm release were at `1.0.0`. The file's own header comment declared "matches the \`version\` field in package.json" as an invariant — but nothing enforced it. **Three-layer defense failure**:

- **Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 readiness audit** missed the file in its 8-file envelope sibling-sweep.
- **`release.yaml` bump-sync set** included `package.json` + `marketplace.json` but not `pinned.yaml`.
- **`tools/validate-docs-drift.js`** cross-checked `oms-*` skill anchors and the `illustrative_libraries` list but never read `skf_version`.

This PR closes all three layers in one commit.

## Changes

### 1. `docs/_data/pinned.yaml:26` — bump `0.10.0` → `1.0.0`

Reflects current live state (v1.0.0 on npm under `--tag latest` since 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z). Header comment expanded to document the two-layer enforcement introduced below.

### 2. `tools/validate-docs-drift.js` — add `checkSkfVersionAgainstPackageJson()`

Reads `package.json.version` and asserts `anchors.skf_version` matches. Runs as part of `npm run quality` (local + pre-commit hook). Not wired into CI `quality.yaml` because `docs:validate-drift` requires the `oh-my-skills` sibling repo checkout, which CI doesn't have — the pre-commit hook is the right enforcement point for these in-tree cross-references.

Error message is actionable — it points at the `release.yaml` step name that should have fired, so a future drift tells the maintainer exactly which sync-on-write path failed:

```
SKF_VERSION_DRIFT: pinned.yaml skf_version "X.Y.Z" !== package.json version "A.B.C" —
bump pinned.yaml or check that release.yaml's "Update pinned.yaml skf_version" step fired
```

Verified with negative-case probe (inject `9.9.9`, confirm validator exits 1, restore).

### 3. `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — new "Update docs/_data/pinned.yaml skf_version" step

New step inserted after the `Update marketplace.json version` step. Uses `sed` with a top-level-key anchor (`^skf_version: "[^"]*"$`), VERSION passed via `env:` per Story 5.4 shell-injection defense-in-depth (not interpolated into the script body), and a post-write `grep` sanity-check that `::error::`s on mismatch. Also adds `docs/_data/pinned.yaml` to the `git add` list at the release commit step so the bump rides with `package.json` + `marketplace.json` in the same release commit.

## Pattern coverage

Closes **Epic 5 retro Commitment 13(a)** "topology claims go stale when the workflow changes" for this specific invariant. Instantiates **Epic 6 retro Commitment 2** "symbolic anchor / cross-file validation" as a tooling guard (not just a prose convention). The two enforcement angles are complementary:

- **Release-side (sync-on-write)** prevents future drift when the version legitimately changes.
- **Validator-side (CI/pre-commit read-verify)** catches any other path that ever touches the file.

## Test plan

- [x] `node tools/validate-docs-drift.js` positive case — passes with `"skf_version 1.0.0 matches package.json"`.
- [x] Negative-case probe — inject `9.9.9`, confirm validator exits 1 with actionable `SKF_VERSION_DRIFT` message; restore.
- [x] `npm run quality` — all 13 subcommands exit 0.
- [x] Pre-commit + commit-msg hooks pass without `--no-verify`.
- [ ] 7 required CI status checks pass on this PR.
- [ ] `release.yaml`'s new step will be exercised on the next release cut (v1.0.1 / v1.1.0). Not validated live in this PR — deferred.

## Not bundled

- Any other Epic 5 retro standing flag (P1 rc dist-tag, P2 audit-evidence hardening, P3 NFR9 automation, P7 `package.json.main`).
- Issue #211 (piped-stdin install infeasibility).
- Any other file that might theoretically version-drift — scope is strictly `skf_version` because that's the documented invariant in `pinned.yaml`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

**Done.**

| Layer | State |
|---|---|
| `docs/_data/pinned.yaml` `skf_version` | `"1.0.0"` ✅ matches `package.json` |
| `tools/validate-docs-drift.js` | now asserts `anchors.skf_version === package.json.version`; positive + negative probes confirmed |
| `.github/workflows/release.yaml` | new "Update docs/_data/pinned.yaml skf_version" step + `pinned.yaml` added to release-commit `git add` set |
| PR #226 | merged at `197c3fc` on 2026-04-25 00:32:20 +04, admin-bypass squash |

Good catch. The defense is now three-layer where it was zero: sync-on-write (release.yaml) + read-verify (validator) + documented invariant (file header). Next release cut will exercise the write-side; pre-commit on any future `pinned.yaml` / `package.json` edit will exercise the read-side.
